Run detect_convergence() obtain k**≥k*k^{**} \ge k^*. Read projected ultimate loss ratio fit_ratio()$summary apply reserving caveat.

title: Diagnostics: maturity, convergence, regime
text: ?detect_maturity, ?detect_convergence, ?detect_regime, ?regime_at, ?maturity_spec, ?fit_ratio, ?backtest. vignette("backtest") — rolling holdout machinery detect_convergence() regime filter case study built . vignette("projection") — fit_ratio() "sa", "ed", "cl" methods.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 장기 건강보험은 한 번 인수한 계약이 수십 년간 유지된다. 그래서 어떤 인수 시점(코호트)의 계약군이 시간이 지나며 손해율을 어떻게 쌓아 가는지를 미리 내다보는 것이 핵심 과제다. lossratio 는 이 문제를 코호트 x 경과기간(cohort x dev) 격자 위에서 다룬다. 코호트(cohort) – 계약을 인수한 시점 (예: 2023년 1월 인수분). 경과기간(development period, dev) – 인수 후 흐른 기간. 각 셀에는 누적 손해(loss)와 누적 보험료(premium)가 들어가고, 손해율은 ratio = loss / premium 로 정의된다. 아직 관측되지 않은 미래 경과기간의 셀을 추정하는 것이 “예측”이며, 이 패키지의 여러 fit_* 함수가 서로 다른 방법으로 그 추정을 수행한다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 패키지에는 합성 experience 데이터가 내장돼 있다. experience 는 도메인 표준어로, 실제 계리 경험통계(experience study)의 셀 단위 기록을 가리킨다. long-format 테이블이며 한 행은 (코호트 x 경과기간 x 그룹) 셀 하나에 대응한다. 핵심 컬럼만 보면 다음과 같다. coverage – 담보(그룹). surgery / ci / cancer / death 네 종목. uy_m – 인수월(underwriting month). 코호트 축의 원본 컬럼. cy_m – 달력월(calendar month). dev_m – 경과월(development month). incr_loss, incr_premium – 증분(기간별) 손해와 보험료. 입력이 증분이라는 점에 주목한다. raw experience 는 보통 “이번 달에 얼마가 발생/경과했는가” 형태로 쌓이기 때문이다. 누적값은 패키지가 내부에서 만든다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: as_triangle() 는 raw experience 를 표준 Triangle 객체로 바꾼다. 검증 + 좌표 표준화 + 집계를 한 번에 수행한다. as_triangle() 의 동작을 풀어 보면 다음과 같다. 필수 컬럼의 존재를 확인하고 expected type 으로 코어션한다, 인구통계 차원을 집계해 제거한다, 누적 컬럼(loss, premium)과 파생 지표(margin, ratio, loss_share, premium_share)를 추가한다, 원본 컬럼명(uy_m, incr_loss 등)을 표준 이름(cohort, dev, loss, premium …)으로 rename 하고, 원본명은 attribute 에 보존한다. 출력 컬럼은 누적이 기본이고 증분은 incr_ 접두사를 붙인다 – loss/incr_loss, premium/incr_premium, ratio/incr_ratio. 이미 누적 삼각형을 갖고 있다면 cell_type = "cumulative" 를 주면 된다. 그러면 as_triangle() 가 코호트별로 차분해 증분을 자동 산출한다. 이후 설명은 한 종목만 떼어 단일 그룹으로 진행하면 출력이 깔끔하다. Triangle 자체를 히트맵으로 볼 수 있다.  대각선 윗쪽(관측 영역)만 값이 차 있고, 아래쪽 빈 곳이 앞으로 예측해야 할 미래 셀이다.
code: tri <- as_triangle(   experience,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   premium  = "incr_premium" ) tri #> shape: (2_664, 18) #> ┌──────────┬───────────┬────────────┬───┬───────────────┬────────────────┐ #> │ coverage ┆ n_cohorts ┆ cohort     ┆ … ┆ premium_share ┆ incr_premium_… │ #> │ <chr>    ┆ <int>     ┆ <date>     ┆ … ┆ <dbl>         ┆ <dbl>          │ #> ├──────────┼───────────┼────────────┼───┼───────────────┼────────────────┤ #> │ ci       ┆ 36        ┆ 2023-01-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.381171      ┆ 0.381171       │ #> │ ci       ┆ 35        ┆ 2023-01-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.380346      ┆ 0.379558       │ #> │ ci       ┆ 34        ┆ 2023-01-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.387352      ┆ 0.401744       │ #> │ ci       ┆ 33        ┆ 2023-01-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.379535      ┆ 0.356118       │ #> │ ci       ┆ 32        ┆ 2023-01-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.377646      ┆ 0.370060       │ #> │ …        ┆ …         ┆ …          ┆ … ┆ …             ┆ …              │ #> │ surgery  ┆ 35        ┆ 2025-10-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.872357      ┆ 0.874228       │ #> │ surgery  ┆ 34        ┆ 2025-10-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.874537      ┆ 0.878680       │ #> │ surgery  ┆ 36        ┆ 2025-11-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.667521      ┆ 0.667521       │ #> │ surgery  ┆ 35        ┆ 2025-11-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.669140      ┆ 0.670648       │ #> │ surgery  ┆ 36        ┆ 2025-12-01 ┆ … ┆ 0.544008      ┆ 0.544008       │ #> └──────────┴───────────┴────────────┴───┴───────────────┴────────────────┘ #> 13 more variables: dev <int>, loss <dbl>, incr_loss <dbl>, premium <dbl>, #>                    incr_premium <dbl>, ratio <dbl>, incr_ratio <dbl>, #>                    margin <dbl>, incr_margin <dbl>, profit <fct>, #>                    incr_profit <fct>, loss_share <dbl>, incr_loss_share <dbl> # 누적 손해/보험료를 가진 경우 as_triangle(df_cumulative,             groups = "coverage", cohort = "uy_m", calendar = "cy_m",             loss = "loss", premium = "premium",             cell_type = "cumulative") tri1 <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "surgery"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   premium  = "incr_premium" ) plot_triangle(tri1, metric = "ratio")

what: 4. Calendar 와 Total – 다른 집계 관점
title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 같은 experience 데이터는 분석 질문에 따라 세 가지 방식으로 집계할 수 있고, 빌더도 셋이다. Calendar 는 코호트를 대각선 위로 합산한다 – 각 행이 모든 인수 코호트에 걸친 하나의 달력 기간이며, 수학적으로 Triangle 의 대각선 합과 동치다. 손해율의 달력 추세나 대각선 효과(calendar-year effect) 점검에 쓴다. t 컬럼은 그룹 내 순차 인덱스(1, 2, 3, …)일 뿐 경과기간이 아니다.  Total 은 두 차원을 모두 합쳐 그룹당 한 값으로 축약한다. 담보별 전체 손해율 비교 같은 포트폴리오 수준 질문에 쓴다. period_from / period_to 로 기간을 맞추면 그룹 간 비교 가능성이 확보된다. 이후 예측 절차는 모두 Triangle 을 입력으로 쓴다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 미래 셀을 추정하려면 “손해가 경과기간에 따라 어떻게 늘어나는가”를 모형으로 표현해야 한다. lossratio 는 두 가지 기본 관점을 쓴다. 곱셈형 – chain ladder (CL). 다음 경과기간의 누적 손해는 현재의 배수다: C_{k+1} = f_k * C_k. 여기서 f_k 가 ATA 인자(age--age factor)이며, 인접한 두 경과기간의 누적 손해 비율로 추정한다. 가법형 – 노출 기반(exposure-driven, ED). 다음 기간의 손해 증분 을 보험료에 비례시킨다: Δloss = g_k * premium. g_k 는 단위 보험료당 손해 강도다. 둘은 서로 보완적이다. 경과 초기에는 누적 손해가 작아 ATA 인자가 크게 출렁이므로 보험료에 닻을 내리는 ED 가 안정적이고, 경과가 무르익으면 ATA 인자가 안정되어 CL 이 코호트의 관측 수준을 잘 보존한다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: Triangle 에서 연속한 두 경과기간을 잇는 Link 객체를 만들면, 위 두 인자를 적합 전에 직접 점검할 수 있다. as_link() 에 loss 만 주면 관측된 ATA 인자를, exposure 까지 주면 ED 강도 g_k 를 담는다 – 워커 레이어에서 보험료 컬럼은 exposure 인자로 받는다. summary(link, model = "ata"|"ed") 는 링크별 mean / median / cv / f(또는 g) 등 인자 통계를 산출한다. 이 통계가 뒤에서 성숙점 검출을 구동한다. 더 깊은 진단은 vignette("getting-started") 를 참고한다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: fit_cl() 은 누적 loss 위에서 Mack(1993) 방식의 chain ladder 적합을 수행한다. summary() 는 코호트별 ultimate 손해와 표준오차(loss_total_se), 변동계수(loss_total_cv)를 보여준다. 표준오차는 Mack 의 분산 분해를 따라 과정오차(process) 와 모수오차(parameter) 의 합으로 산출된다. $full 슬롯에는 관측 + 예측 전 영역의 셀별 결과가 들어 있다. 투영 곡선을 보면 코호트별 관측(실선)과 예측(점선)이 이어진다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: fit_ed() 는 같은 삼각형을 가법형 ED 모형으로 적합한다. 점 예측(loss_proj)은 CL 과 ED 가 거의 같다 – 둘 다 같은 데이터를 설명하기 때문이다. 차이는 분산이 어떻게 쌓이는가 에 있다. CL 은 배수 f 의 제곱으로 분산을 증폭시키고, ED 는 보험료에 비례한 증분을 더해 간다. 그래서 초기 경과기간에서 ED 의 표준오차가 더 차분하다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: fit_sa() 는 두 관점을 단계 적응형(stage-adaptive, SA) 으로 합성한다. 성숙점(maturity point) 이전 경과기간은 ED 로, 이후는 CL 로 적합한다. 성숙점은 ATA 인자가 충분히 안정된 경과기간으로, 내부에서 자동 검출된다 (2-pass). 직관은 단순하다 – ATA 인자가 아직 출렁이는 초기 구간은 보험료에 닻을 내리고(ED), 안정된 이후 구간은 코호트 자신의 발전을 따른다(CL). SA 는 이 패키지가 장기 손해율 예측의 기본 작업마로 삼는 방법이다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: fit_bf() 는 외부 사전(prior) 손해율 을 끌어들인다. 아직 발현되지 않은 미래 부분은 데이터가 아니라 prior 가 채운다. L̂iult=Liobs+(1−qi)⋅ELRi⋅Eiult \hat L^{ult}_i = L^{obs}_i + (1 - q_i)\cdot \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E^{ult}_i q_i 는 코호트 의 발현 비율(L^{obs} / L^{ult, CL})이다. 즉 BF 는 이미 발현된 부분은 데이터, 아직 안 나온 부분은 prior 로 채우는 신뢰도(credibility) 혼합이다. prior 인자는 필수다. prior 를 코호트마다 다르게 주거나(데이터프레임), 분포 prior(elr_se 열 포함)로 줄 수도 있다. 데이터가 거의 없는 초기 코호트일수록 결과가 prior 쪽으로 끌린다 – 그것이 BF 를 쓰는 이유다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: fit_cc() 는 BF 와 같은 구조이되, 사전 손해율을 외부에서 받지 않고 데이터를 풀링해 추정 한다. ELR̂=∑iLiobs∑iEiult⋅qi \widehat{\mathrm{ELR}} =   \frac{\sum_i L^{obs}_i}{\sum_i E^{ult}_i \cdot q_i} prior 인자가 없는 점만 빼면 호출과 출력은 BF 와 같다. prior 를 따로 구할 근거가 없을 때, 자기 포트폴리오가 스스로 ELR 을 말하게 하는 방법이다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 방법을 매번 다른 함수로 부르는 대신, fit_loss() 하나로 method 인자만 바꿔 호출할 수 있다. method 는 "ed"(기본), "cl", "sa", "bf", "cc" 중 하나다. "bf"/"cc" 는 prior 등 추가 인자를 ... 로 넘긴다. fit_premium() 는 보험료 측을 따로 적합하는 짝 함수다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: fit_ratio() 는 손해 측과 보험료 측을 각각 적합한 뒤 둘을 결합해 손해율 을 산출하는 합성(composition) 레이어다. $full 에는 loss_proj / premium_proj / ratio_proj 와 손해율 표준오차(ratio_se)·신뢰구간(ratio_ci_lo/ratio_ci_hi)이 함께 들어 있다. 손해율 표준오차는 se_method 로 정한다 – "fixed"(기본)는 보험료를 알려진 값으로 취급하고, "delta"는 보험료의 불확실성과 손해-보험료 상관까지 델타법으로 반영한다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 적합 전후로 삼각형의 구조를 점검하는 진단 함수들이 있다. 성숙점 – ATA 인자가 안정되는 경과기간. SA 의 단계 전환점이기도 하다. 수렴점(convergence point) – 예측 손해율이 더 이상 의미 있게 움직이지 않는 경과기간. 누적 손해율의 분모가 자라며 신호를 감쇠시키는 관성(inertia) 을 이중 기준으로 걸러 검출한다. regime – 인수 코호트 사이의 구조적 변화. 보험료·약관·인수 가이드라인 변경 등이 특정 시점 이후 코호트의 손해 양상을 바꿨는지를 탐지한다. experience 의 surgery 종목에는 2024 년 중 합성 regime change 가 심어져 있어, 위 결과에서 해당 변화 시점이 잡힌다.
code: detect_maturity(tri1) #> Key: <coverage> #>    coverage ata_from change ata_link     mean   median       wt        cv #>      <char>    <num>  <num>   <char>    <num>    <num>    <num>     <num> #> 1:  surgery        3      4      3-4 1.434507 1.400098 1.417706 0.1053282 #>           f       f_se        rse    sigma n_cohorts n_valid n_inf n_nan #>       <num>      <num>      <num>    <num>     <num>   <num> <num> <num> #> 1: 1.417706 0.02651852 0.01870522 1372.883        33      33     0     0 #>    valid_ratio #>          <num> #> 1:           1 detect_convergence(tri1) #> <Convergence> #>   method     : tail #>   conv_k     : NA #>   mat_k      : 4 #>   dev_max    : 36 #>   candidates : 31 #>   passes : #>     window :  0/31 (drift_window < 0.01  & dispersion < 0.15) #>     tail   :  0/31 (drift_tail   < 0.01  & dispersion < 0.15)  <- method #>     slope  :  1/31 (|slope|      < 0.001 & dispersion < 0.15) #>     all    :  0/31 (window AND tail AND slope) reg <- detect_regime(tri) reg$changes #>    coverage     change regime_id pre_value post_value magnitude #>      <char>     <Date>     <int>     <num>      <num>     <num> #> 1:  surgery 2024-07-01         2 0.9065895  0.5479919 0.3585976

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: backtest() 는 최근 달력 대각선 몇 개를 가린 뒤 다시 적합하고, 가려진 실제값과 예측값을 비교해 모형의 정확도를 측정한다. target 은 "ratio"/"loss"/"premium" 중 하나이며, 각각 fit_ratio/fit_loss/fit_premium 를 내부에서 호출한다. 결과의 /E error 표로 어느 경과기간·대각선에서 모형이 빗나갔는지 볼 수 있다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 모든 주요 객체는 두 갈래의 그림을 지원한다. plot(x) – 궤적/진단 차트 (코호트별 전개 곡선, regime PCA 등). plot_triangle(x) – 삼각형 히트맵 (셀별 값 또는 셀 상태).  plot_triangle() 의 label_style 은 셀 라벨을 값("value")·변동계수 ("cv")·표준오차("se")·신뢰구간("ci")으로 바꾼다. plot() 의 type·region 등 세부 인자는 각 클래스의 도움말(?plot.CLFit 등)을 참고한다. CL / ED / SA / BF / CC / Premium 적합 결과 모두 같은 방식으로 plot() 과 plot_triangle() 을 지원하므로, 어떤 방법을 쓰든 시각화 인터페이스는 동일하다.

title: lossratio 시작하기
text: 이 문서는 핵심 작업 흐름을 한 번에 따라가는 것이 목적이다. 각 주제의 방법론적 배경과 세부 옵션은 다음을 참고한다. ?as_triangle, ?fit_cl, ?fit_sa, ?fit_ratio 등 함수별 도움말. vignette("projection") – 예측 방법 선택과 방법론 상세. vignette("diagnostics") – 성숙점·수렴점·regime 진단 심화. vignette("backtest") – 검증 절차. vignette("bootstrap-se-decomposition") – 부트스트랩 표준오차 분해.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: long-term health insurance policy, underwritten, stays force decades. central task therefore look ahead see group contracts written given point time (cohort) accumulates loss ratio years pass. lossratio frames problem cohort x development-period (cohort x dev) grid. cohort – time contract underwritten (e.g. January 2023 business). development period (dev) – time elapsed since underwriting. cell holds cumulative loss (loss) cumulative premium (premium), loss ratio defined ratio = loss / premium. Estimating cells future development periods yet observed act projection, various fit_* functions package perform estimate different ways.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: package ships synthetic experience dataset. term experience domain standard, referring cell-level records actuarial experience study. key columns : coverage – line business (group). Four products: surgery, ci, cancer, death. uy_m – underwriting month. source column cohort axis. cy_m – calendar month. dev_m – development month. incr_loss, incr_premium – incremental (per-period) loss premium. Note input incremental: raw experience usually accumulated form “much incurred / earned month”. cumulative values constructed inside package.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: as_triangle() turns raw experience standard Triangle object. performs validation, coordinate standardisation, aggregation one call. takes original column names (uy_m, incr_loss, …) renames standard names (cohort, dev, loss, premium, …), preserving originals attributes. output columns cumulative default, incremental columns carrying incr_ prefix – loss/incr_loss, premium/incr_premium, ratio/incr_ratio. already hold cumulative triangle, pass cell_type = "cumulative". as_triangle() differences within cohort recover increments automatically. rest walkthrough uses single product output stays compact. triangle can viewed heatmap.  upper-left region (observed area) filled; empty lower-right cells future cells still projected.

when loss / premium are already cumulative as_triangle(df_cumulative,             groups = "coverage", cohort = "uy_m", calendar = "cy_m",             loss = "loss", premium = "premium",             cell_type = "cumulative") tri1 <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "surgery"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   premium  = "incr_premium" ) plot_triangle(tri1, metric = "ratio")

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: project future cells, way “loss grows development time” must expressed model. lossratio uses two basic views. Multiplicative – chain ladder (CL). cumulative loss next development period multiple current one: C_{k+1} = f_k * C_k. f_k age--age factor, estimated ratio cumulative loss two adjacent development periods. Additive – exposure-driven (ED). loss increment next period made proportional premium: Δloss = g_k * premium. g_k loss intensity per unit premium. two complementary. Early development cumulative loss small, age--age factor swings widely; anchoring premium makes exposure-driven view stable. development matures age--age factor settles, chain ladder preserves cohort’s observed level well.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_cl() performs Mack (1993) chain ladder fit cumulative loss. summary() reports ultimate loss per cohort standard error (loss_total_se) coefficient variation (loss_total_cv). standard error follows Mack’s variance decomposition, computed sum process error parameter error. $full slot holds cell-level result observed projected regions. projection curves connect cohort’s observed values (solid) projection (dashed).

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_ed() fits triangle additive exposure-driven model. point projection (loss_proj) nearly identical chain ladder exposure-driven – explain data. difference lies variance accumulates. Chain ladder amplifies variance square multiplier f, exposure-driven model adds premium-proportional increments. result exposure-driven standard error calmer early development periods.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_sa() blends two views stage-adaptive (SA) way: development periods maturity point fit exposure-driven model, periods chain ladder. maturity point development period age--age factor settled sufficiently, detected automatically inside fit (two-pass). intuition simple: early region, age--age factor still swings, anchors premium (ED); settled region thereafter follows cohort’s development (CL). Stage adaptation package’s default workhorse long-term loss-ratio projection.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_bf() brings external prior loss ratio. portion loss yet emerged filled data prior. L̂iult=Liobs+(1−qi)⋅ELRi⋅Eiult \hat L^{ult}_i = L^{obs}_i + (1 - q_i)\cdot \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E^{ult}_i q_i emergence ratio cohort (L^{obs} / L^{ult, CL}). words Bornhuetter-Ferguson credibility blend fills already-emerged part data -yet-emerged part prior. prior argument required. prior can vary cohort (data frame) distributional prior (elr_se column). younger cohort – less data – result pulled toward prior; precisely reason use Bornhuetter-Ferguson.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_cc() structure Bornhuetter-Ferguson, except prior loss ratio supplied externally – estimated pooling data. ELR̂=∑iLiobs∑iEiult⋅qi \widehat{\mathrm{ELR}} =   \frac{\sum_i L^{obs}_i}{\sum_i E^{ult}_i \cdot q_i} Apart absence prior argument, call output match Bornhuetter-Ferguson. method use external basis prior let portfolio speak ELR.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: Rather calling different function method, fit_loss() lets switch method single method argument. method one "ed" (default), "cl", "sa", "bf", "cc". "bf" / "cc" extra arguments prior forwarded .... fit_premium() companion function fits premium side separately.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: fit_ratio() composition layer: fits loss side premium side separately, combines produce loss ratio. $full holds loss_proj / premium_proj / ratio_proj together loss-ratio standard error (ratio_se) confidence interval (ratio_ci_lo / ratio_ci_hi). loss-ratio standard error governed se_method: "fixed" (default) treats premium known value, "delta" carries uncertainty premium loss-premium correlation delta method.

what: Calendar and Total – the other aggregation frameworks
title: Getting started with lossratio
text: long-format experience can aggregated three ways depending question. as_triangle() keeps axes (cohort x dev) projection; two siblings collapse one axes. as_calendar() collapses cohorts onto diagonal, giving one row per calendar period – diagonal sum triangle. Use calendar-time trend diagonal-effect analysis.  t column sequential index within group (1, 2, 3, …), time-series convention. development period. as_total() collapses axes one value per group, useful portfolio-level comparison. period_from / period_to arguments bound fixed window groups stay comparable.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: Triangle fit sits Link object, per-link factor table built as_link(). intermediate object feeds chain ladder exposure-driven model. single-variable mode (loss ) Link carries observed age--age factors. Supplying exposure adds exposure-driven intensity intensity = loss_delta / premium_from. summary() method computes per-link statistics – mean / median / weighted factor, coefficient variation, WLS standard error – drive maturity detection. factor-level helpers fit_ata() fit_intensity() wrap machinery return per-link estimates ($full projection).

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: diagnostic functions inspecting triangle’s structure fitting. Maturity point – development period age--age factor settles. also stage-switch point stage-adaptive method. Convergence point – development period beyond projected loss ratio longer moves meaningfully. denominator cumulative loss ratio grows dampens signal – inertia effect – dual criterion used filter . Regime – structural change underwriting cohorts. detects whether premium, policy-wording, underwriting-guideline change altered loss behaviour cohorts certain point. surgery product experience synthetic regime change planted 2024, result picks change point.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: backtest() hides latest calendar diagonals, refits model, compares withheld actuals projection measure model’s accuracy. target one "ratio" / "loss" / "premium", calling fit_ratio / fit_loss / fit_premium internally. resulting /E error tables show development periods diagonals model missed.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: Every major object supports two families plots. plot(x) – trajectory / diagnostic charts (per-cohort development curves, regime PCA, ). plot_triangle(x) – triangular heatmaps (cell values cell status).  label_style argument plot_triangle() switches cell labels value ("value"), coefficient variation ("cv"), standard error ("se"), confidence interval ("ci"). detailed arguments plot() – type, region, – see class’s help page (?plot.CLFit friends). CL / ED / SA / BF / CC / premium fit results support plot() plot_triangle() way, visualisation interface identical regardless method.

title: Getting started with lossratio
text: tutorial follows core workflow one pass. methodological background detailed options topic, see: ?as_triangle, ?fit_cl, ?fit_sa, ?fit_ratio per-function help pages. vignette("projection") – projection methods depth. vignette("diagnostics") – maturity, convergence, regime detection. vignette("backtest") – validation procedure. vignette("bootstrap-se-decomposition") – bootstrap standard errors variance decomposition.

title: 예측 방법론
text: 장기건강보험 손해율은 매일 다루는 task 다 — 코호트별 손해율 패턴 분석, 미래 손해율 ult 예측, 실적이 예상과 얼마나 일치하는지 모니터링. 그러나 기존 reserving 도구의 방법론적 기반은 대부분 손해보험(P&C) 영역에서 발전했고, 장기건강보험의 다음과 같은 고유 특성은 직접 반영되지 않는다. 분모효과(denominator effect)와 관성(inertia) — 누적 손해율 자체가 경과 기간이 누적될수록 분모가 자동으로 자라면서 신호가 감쇠된다. 다회성 보험금 — 입원·수술·통원 등은 한 피보험자가 여러 차례 보험금을 받는다. 한 코호트의 ultimate frequency 가 1 을 초과할 수 있다. 위험보험료 분리 — 한국의 장기보험료는 위험보험료 + 저축보험료 + 부가보험료의 합이다. 손해율의 진짜 분모는 위험보험료만이다. 평탄화 보험료 — 비갱신형 상품은 가입 시 평균값으로 산출된 level premium 을 전 기간 동일 부과한다. 따라서 charged premium 자체는 premium 가 아니다. 실제 period risk premium 은 위험률(t) x 가입금액 x 유지율(t) 로 외부 산출 후 입력해야 한다. 체제 변화(regime change) — 약관 개정·요율 조정·채널 mix 변화·인수 기준 변경 등이 코호트 단위로 손해율 양상을 바꾼다. lossratio 는 P&C 영역에서 발전한 reserving 방법론 — Mack 1993 chain ladder, Bornhuetter-Ferguson 1972, Cape Cod (Stanard 1985), Bühlmann-Straub 1970 credibility, Sherman 1984 의 tail 외삽 — 을 위 도메인 issue 에 맞춰 재구성한 framework 다. 학술적 lineage 를 존중하되, 장기건강보험 실무에서 바로 쓸 수 있는 도구가 목적이다.

title: 예측 방법론
text: 장기 손해율 추정의 방법론적 뿌리는 다음의 P&C reserving 흐름이다. 이후 모든 내용을 끌고 가는 핵심 idea 두 가지: Chain ladder (Mack 1993) — 누적 손해 CkC_k 의 Markov 곱셈 recursion: Ck+1=fk⋅CkC_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C_k fk=∑iCi,k+1/∑iCi,kf_k = \sum_i C_{,k+1} / \sum_i C_{,k}. Cape Cod / Bühlmann-Straub — 손해를 premium(= volume)으로 anchoring. ELR̂=∑iLi/∑iπi\widehat{ELR} = \sum_i L_i / \sum_i \pi_i 의 single ratio 로 ult 산출. 장기건강보험에 적용 시 둘 다 부분적으로 작동하지만, 모두 cracks 가 있다. chain ladder 는 초기 dev 에 약하고, Cape Cod 는 premium 의 발전 양상을 처리하지 못한다. 둘 다 각자 한 paradigm 의 한계를 갖는다 — 패키지가 하나가 아니라 다섯 가지 방법을 제공하는 이유가 바로 이것이다.
code: 1967  Bühlmann credibility           -- experience rating 의 수학적 형식화 1970  Bühlmann-Straub                -- premium-varying credibility                                        (volume-weighted estimator) 1972  Bornhuetter-Ferguson           -- prior + observed loss 결합 1984  Sherman                        -- chain ladder tail factor 외삽 1985  Stanard (Cape Cod)             -- Bühlmann-Straub 의 reserving 응용 1993  Mack                           -- chain ladder 의 distribution-free MSE

title: 예측 방법론
text: lossratio 의 모든 추정은 다음 세 양 위에서 이루어진다. 세 양 모두 cohort x dev grid 위에서 발전하는 관측치(stochastic observable)다. premium 는 고정된 underwriting volume 이 아니라 위험률 x 가입금액 x 유지율의 dev 따라 변동하는 양이다 — Mack 1993 의 volume measure, Bühlmann-Straub 1970 의 natural weight 와 같은 의미다. 코호트 ii, dev kk 에 대하여 예측 방법들이 쓰는 표기는 다음과 같다. Ci,kLC^L_{,k} — 누적 손해액 Ci,kPC^P_{,k} — 누적 위험보험료 fk=Ck+1L/CkLf_k = C^L_{k+1} / C^L_k — ATA 인자(age--age factor) gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k — 노출 기반(exposure-driven, ED) 강도 성숙점(maturity point) k*k^* — 그룹에서 fkf_k 가 안정화되는 dev (CV / RSE 임계값으로 탐지) 이하 모든 예시는 간결성을 위해 surgery 그룹만 사용한다 — 모든 절차는 다중 그룹 입력에도 그대로 일반화된다.
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) tri <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "surgery"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   premium  = "incr_premium" )

title: 예측 방법론
text: 직접 추정 세 method 는 데이터로부터 인자를 추정해 전방으로 연결한다. 순서 ed -> cl -> sa 가 방법론적 진화를 그대로 표현한다 — primitive(ED) -> classical(CL) -> composition(SA).

title: 예측 방법론
text: ED 는 모든 미래 증분을 premium(위험보험료)을 분모로 하여 예측한다. Ĉ,k+1L=Ĉ,kL+gk⋅Ci,kP \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} = \hat{C}^L_{,k} + g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} ED 가 기본값인 이유는 unconditional safe baseline 이기 때문이다 — 성숙점이나 regime 검출에 의존하지 않으며, 초기 dev 의 ATA 인자 변동에도 강건하다. 초기 dev 의 작은 CkC_k 가 분모에 들어가지 않으므로, 초기 fkf_k 가 일으키는 추정량 과대 변동에 영향받지 않는다. 대가는 코호트 동질성 가정이다. pooled intensity gkg_k 는 코호트들이 단위 보험료당 손해 수준에서 대체로 동질적이라 가정한다. 코호트-레벨 drift 가 있으면 추정이 pooled 평균으로 편향되어 post-change 코호트를 -project 할 수 있다 — 명시적 필터는 regime 인자다. 언제 사용: 코호트 동질성이 그럴듯한 baseline 으로; chain ladder 가 이점이 없는 short-tail 상품; ATA 인자가 전 link 에서 신뢰할 수 없는 sparse 데이터.

title: 예측 방법론
text: CL 은 고전적 Mack (1993) 모형이다. Ĉ,k+1L=fk⋅Ĉ,kL \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot \hat{C}^L_{,k} 코호트 자기 누적 손해가 anchor 역할을 하므로 코호트-레벨 drift 가 명시적 regime 검출 없이 자연스럽게 propagation 된다. 대가는 ED 의 거울상이다 — 초기 fkf_k 에 노이즈가 있을 때 변동성이 크다. 작은 분모가 link 오차를 증폭하기 때문이다. fit_ratio() 안에서 CL method 는 손해와 보험료를 모두 — 각자 자기 컬럼에 대한 chain ladder 로 — 전방 추정하고 delta method 로 손해율 불확실성을 계산한다. 손해 lane 단독은 fit_cl(tri) 과 동등하다. 언제 사용: ATA 인자가 안정된 후; 코호트의 관측 경로가 추정의 anchor 가 되어야 하는 코호트-레벨 drift 시나리오; 규제 당국이 문서화 목적으로 고전적 Mack 형식을 요구하는 적립 작업.

title: 예측 방법론
text: SA 는 성숙점 이전을 ED 로, 이후를 CL 로 합성한다. fkf_k 가 초반에는 변동성이 크고 후반에는 안정적이며, gkg_k 는 그 반대로 움직인다는 사실을 활용한다. Ĉ,k+1L={Ĉ,kL+gk⋅Ci,kPk<k*(성숙점 이전: ED)fk⋅Ĉ,kLk≥k*(성숙점 이후: CL) \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} \;=\; \begin{cases} \hat{C}^L_{,k} + g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} & k < k^* \quad \text{(성숙점 이전: ED)} \\ f_k \cdot \hat{C}^L_{,k}              & k \ge k^* \quad \text{(성숙점 이후: CL)} \end{cases} 성숙점 이전 SA 는 손해 추정치를 보험료 volume 에 anchor 하여, 초기 fkf_k 가 노이즈로 폭주하는 고전 CL 의 문제를 회피한다. 성숙점 이후 SA 는 코호트 자체의 관측 수준을 보존하여, 순수 ED 가 꼬리에서 모든 코호트를 평균으로 끌어당기는 문제를 회피한다. 언제 사용: 초기 dev 의 변동성(ED phase)과 후기 dev 의 코호트-레벨 drift(CL phase)를 둘 다 처리해야 하는 장기-tail 포트폴리오; 최근 코호트(미성숙 데이터)와 오래된 코호트(성숙 데이터)가 혼재하는 경우; 성숙점 이전·이후의 구조적 차이가 있는 건강보험 코호트(예: 면책기간 전환).

title: 예측 방법론
예측 방법론
text: ED, CL, SA 는 모두 인자를 데이터로부터 추정한다. 관측 triangle 이 빈약할 때 — 미성숙 코호트, 또는 요율 변경 직후 코호트 — 그 추정은 불안정할 수 있다. prior-anchored family 는 기대 손해율(ELR)을 이미 발현된 손해와 결합한다. Ult=Llatest+(1−1LDF)⋅π⋅ELR \text{Ult} = L_{\text{latest}}   + \left(1 - \tfrac{1}{\text{LDF}}\right) \cdot \pi \cdot \text{ELR} 두 method 는 ELR 이 어디서 오는가 에서만 다르다.

title: 예측 방법론
text: BF 는 ELR 을 외부 입력으로 받는다. 이미 발현된 손해 LlatestL_{\text{latest}} 는 그대로 두고, 미발현 부분 (1−1/LDF)(1 - 1/\text{LDF}) 만 prior 로 채운다. 코호트가 성숙할수록 데이터 항이 우세해지고 prior 는 사라진다 — BF 는 chain ladder 의 답으로 매끄럽게 수렴한다. prior 인자는 그룹별 data.frame 도 받으며, elr_se 컬럼을 함께 넘기면 prior 를 고정점이 아닌 분포(distribution prior)로 다룬다.
code: fit_bf(tri, prior = 0.7) #> <BFFit> #> method        : bf  #> loss          : loss  #> premium       : premium  #> alpha         : 1  #> sigma_method  : locf  #> recent        : all  #> regime        : none #> groups        : coverage  #> cohorts (n)   : 36  #> prior         : scalar elr = 0.7  #> ci_type       : analytical

title: 예측 방법론
text: CC 는 BF 와 같은 결합 형태를 갖지만 ELR 을 데이터 자체에서 추정한다 — 코호트 전반의 payout-weighted pooled ratio. pricing 또는 industry 맥락이 일관된 단일 ELR target 을 시사하고 외부 prior 가 없을 때 자연 스러운 선택이다.
code: fit_cc(tri) #> <CCFit> #> method        : cc  #> loss          : loss  #> premium       : premium  #> alpha         : 1  #> sigma_method  : locf  #> recent        : all  #> regime        : none #> groups        : coverage  #> cohorts (n)   : 36  #> pooled ELR    : #>   surgery : 1.3558 #> ci_type       : analytical

title: 예측 방법론
text: ED, CC, BF 는 단위 보험료당 손해 신호를 집계하는 방식이 다르다. ED — link 별 gkg_k: dev-granular, link 별 cohort-uniform. CC — 코호트 단위 single ELR: cohort-uniform, dev-aggregated. BF — 외부 prior: 데이터 추정 자체를 회피. 다섯 method 가 함께 P&C reserving trinity 를 장기건강보험에 맞춰 재구성하며, 세 aggregation 축이 각자 고유한 use case 를 갖는다.

title: 예측 방법론
text: fit_cl() 은 단일 값 컬럼에 대한 전용 chain ladder 적합 함수다. 손해와 보험료를 동시에 추정해 손해율을 산출하는 fit_ratio() 과 달리, fit_cl() 은 하나의 누적 지표를 전방으로 추정하고 코호트별 Mack 방식 표준오차를 함께 계산한다. 이는 고전적 P&C reserving use case 다 — 보고기간 미종료 사고연도의 ultimate 지급/발생 손해 추정. P&C 배경 실무자는 익숙한 Mack workflow 를 그대로 알아볼 수 있다. fit_cl() 은 인접 dev 의 누적 손해 비 fk=Ck+1L/CkLf_k = C^L_{k+1} / C^L_k — ATA 인자 — 를 link 별로 선택한 뒤 누적 곱으로 연결해 각 코호트의 ultimate 까지 추정한다. 점 추정에 더해 Mack 공식은 추정 분산을 프로세스 분산과 모수 분산으로 분해한다. loss_proc_se — 프로세스 분산. σk2\sigma^2_k (경과 기간별 잔차 링크 분산) 으로부터 도출. loss_param_se — 모수 분산. 선택된 ATA 인자 f̂k\hat{f}_k 의 불확실성으로부터 도출. loss_total_se — 총 표준오차, loss_proc_se2+loss_param_se2\sqrt{\mathrm{loss\_proc\_se}^2 + \mathrm{loss\_param\_se}^2}. loss_total_cv — 변동계수, loss_total_se / loss_proj.   plot_triangle() 은 코호트 x dev 셀을 히트맵으로 표시하며, 관측된 셀과 추정된 셀을 구분하고 label_style 로 셀별 CV / SE / CI 를 표시한다.

title: 예측 방법론
text: 마지막 관측 경과 기간에서도 손해가 여전히 발달 중인 triangle 의 경우, 외삽한 tail 인자(tail factor)로 ultimate 를 추정한다. 외삽은 선택된 ATA 인자에 대해 log⁡(fk−1)∼k\log(f_k - 1) \sim k 회귀 — Sherman (1984) log-linear tail — 를 적합한 뒤, 외삽된 fkf_k 의 누적 곱만큼 추정 범위를 연장한다. 기본값은 비활성(tail = FALSE)이다.
code: fit_cl(tri, tail = TRUE)         # 로그 선형 외삽 #> <CLFit> #> method      : mack  #> loss        : loss  #> weight      : none  #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: locf  #> recent      : all  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> tail_factor : 1.188138  #> groups      : coverage  #> periods     : 36 fit_cl(tri, tail = 1.025)        # 또는 명시적 tail 인자 값 #> <CLFit> #> method      : mack  #> loss        : loss  #> weight      : none  #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: locf  #> recent      : all  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> tail_factor : 1.025  #> groups      : coverage  #> periods     : 36

title: 예측 방법론
text: Mack 분산은 모든 발달 링크에서 σk\sigma_k 가 필요한데, 마지막 링크에서는 직접 추정이 불가능하다. sigma_method 가 외삽 방식을 결정한다. 장기 건강보험 권장: "locf" (default) 또는 "mack" (학술 보고).
code: fit_cl(tri, sigma_method = "loglinear") #> <CLFit> #> method      : mack  #> loss        : loss  #> weight      : none  #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: loglinear  #> recent      : all  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> tail_factor : 1  #> groups      : coverage  #> periods     : 36

title: 예측 방법론
text: fit_ratio() 은 L/P 의 표준오차를 두 가지 방식으로 산출한다. premium 불확실성이 어떻게 들어가는지가 차이다. se_method = "fixed" (default) — premium 을 고정(비확률)으로 취급, SE(L/P)≈SE(L)/P\mathrm{SE}(L/P) \approx \mathrm{SE}(L)/P. 엄밀히는 Var(P) = 0, Cov(L,P) = 0 을 가정한 delta method 의 degenerate case. se_method = "delta" — premium 불확실성과 손해-premium 상관계수 rho 를 반영한 full delta method: Var(L/P)≈Var(L)P2+L2Var(P)P4−2ρLSE(L)SE(P)P3 \mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{P^2}   + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(P)}{P^4}   - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P)}{P^3} 표준오차 추정에는 상호 보완적인 두 path 가 있다. 로 closed-form 표준오차. 빠르고 분포 가정 free. Bootstrap — 잔차 paradigm (cell / link / parametric) 별 forward simulation 으로 분포 형태까지 추정. residual 종류가 process variance scale 과 forward sim 모델을 함께 결정 (paradigm matching). 두 path 의 SE 가 현저히 다를 경우 모델 misspecification 신호다. 평소 sanity check 으로 둘 다 산출해 비교하는 것이 권장된다.

title: 예측 방법론
text: method = "sa" 에서는 성숙점 k*k^* 가 ED -> CL 전환 지점이 된다. fit_ratio() / fit_loss() 의 default 는 maturity = "auto" 로, detect_maturity() 를 default 임계값으로 내부 호출한다. 그 외 형태: maturity = NULL — 탐지 비활성 (SA 가 아닌 method 나 fit_ata() 단독 호출에서만 의미). maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.05, min_run = 2L) — 사용자 임계값을 캡처한 지연 spec. fit 시점에 detect_maturity() 가 실행됨 (backtest() 에서 leakage-safe). maturity = detect_maturity(tri, ...) — 미리 산출된 Maturity 객체. 다중 refit 에서 고정. maturity = maturity_at(coverage = "surgery", change = 4) — 그룹별 k*k^* 수동 지정 (예: 회사 표준값). 같은 지연-spec / 사전-산출 / 수동 지정 패턴이 fit_cl() 에도 적용되며, 거기서는 선택된 ATA 인자가 변동성이 클 때 추정을 성숙 영역으로 제한하는 데 쓰인다.

title: 예측 방법론
text: ED 가 기본값인 이유는 unconditional safe baseline 이기 때문이다 — 성숙점이나 regime 검출 없이도 동작하며 초기 dev 변동에도 보수적이다. 다른 방법들은 ED 의 동질성 가정이 비현실적이거나, 빈약한 triangle 에서 추정한 인자보다 외부 anchor 가 나을 때 정당화된다. 실무: "ed" 로 시작한다 (default). 이후 민감도 점검을 위해 "cl" 과 "sa" 를 함께 실행한다. 셋이 모두 일치하면 추정은 견고 하다. 결과가 갈라지면 성숙점 탐지, regime 필터, 기저 ATA 인자를 점검한다. 코호트가 너무 미성숙해 데이터 기반 방법을 신뢰하기 어려운 경우 "bf" / "cc" 를 고려한다.
code: 기본은 "ed" — pooled g_k x 코호트 보험료. 성숙점 / regime 의존 없음.  ED 의 가정이 한계에 부딪힐 때 다른 방법으로 전환:   ├── 코호트-레벨 drift (체제 변화, 인수 / 보장 변경)   │     → "cl"  (코호트 자기 cum_loss 가 drift 를 anchor)   ├── 초기 dev 변동 + 후기 코호트 drift 둘 다인 long-tail   │     → "sa"  (성숙점 이전 ED, 이후 CL)   ├── 모든 코호트가 이미 성숙, 초기 dev 변동 없음   │     → "cl"  (ED 영역 불필요)   ├── 미성숙 / 요율 변경 직후 코호트, 외부 ELR 보유   │     → "bf"  (외부 prior 를 발현 손해와 결합)   └── 일관된 단일 ELR target, 외부 prior 없음         → "cc"  (데이터 pooled ELR, BF 와 같은 결합 형태)

title: 예측 방법론
text: Bornhuetter, R. L. Ferguson, R. E. (1972). actuary IBNR. Proceedings Casualty Actuarial Society, 59, 181-195. Bühlmann, H. (1967). Experience rating credibility. ASTIN Bulletin, 4(3), 199-207. Bühlmann, H. Straub, E. (1970). Glaubwürdigkeit für Schadensätze. Bulletin Swiss Association Actuaries, 70, 111-133. Clark, D. R. (2003). LDF curve-fitting stochastic reserving: maximum likelihood approach. CAS Forum, Fall 2003. Mack, T. (1993). Distribution-free calculation standard error chain ladder reserve estimates. ASTIN Bulletin, 23(2), 213-225. Sherman, R. E. (1984). Extrapolating, smoothing, interpolating development factors. Proceedings Casualty Actuarial Society, 71, 122-155. Stanard, J. N. (1985). simulation test prediction errors loss reserve estimation techniques. Proceedings Casualty Actuarial Society, 72, 124-153.

title: 예측 방법론
text: vignette("getting-started") — 패키지 시작 가이드 (데이터 -> Triangle/Link -> 적합). vignette("diagnostics") — 성숙점·수렴점·regime 검출과 필터링. vignette("backtest") — 대각선 hold-으로 모델 검증. vignette("bootstrap-se-decomposition") — 부트스트랩 기반 SE 분해.

title: Projection methods
text: Loss ratio daily task long-term health insurance: analysing cohort-level patterns, projecting ultimate outcomes, monitoring realised experience expectations. Existing reserving toolkits mostly trace back property casualty (P&C) origins, following long-term health characteristics directly captured: Denominator effect inertia — Cumulative loss ratio’s denominator grows mechanically development, dampening signal recent experience. Recurrent claims — Hospitalisation, surgery (grade 1-5), outpatient coverage allow single insured file multiple claims; cumulative claim count can exceed insured count. Risk premium decomposition — Korea, long-term insurance premium splits risk premium + savings premium + loading premium. actuarially meaningful denominator loss ratio risk premium portion alone. Levelled premium — Non-renewable contracts charge level premium computed issue lifetime average. charged premium premium; period risk premium (morbidity rate x sum insured x persistency) must constructed externally passed . Regime change — Product redesigns, rate revisions, channel-mix shifts, underwriting updates introduce cohort-level structural breaks loss-ratio dynamics. lossratio transplants core P&C reserving methodology — Mack (1993) chain ladder, Bornhuetter-Ferguson (1972), Cape Cod (Stanard 1985), Bühlmann-Straub (1970) credibility, Sherman (1984) tail extrapolation — adapts piece long-term health issues . academic lineage preserved; goal tool domain practitioners can use immediately.

title: Projection methods
text: methodological roots long-term loss-ratio estimation lie following P&C reserving thread: Two core ideas drive everything follows: Chain ladder (Mack 1993) — Markov multiplicative recursion cumulative loss CkC_k: Ck+1=fk⋅CkC_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C_k fk=∑iCi,k+1/∑iCi,kf_k = \sum_i C_{,k+1} / \sum_i C_{,k}. Cape Cod / Bühlmann-Straub — Loss anchored premium (volume). ELR̂=∑iLi/∑iπi\widehat{ELR} = \sum_i L_i / \sum_i \pi_i yields single ratio drives ultimate estimation. apply partially long-term health, cracks: Chain ladder weak early; Cape Cod track developing premium. paradigm incomplete — exactly package offers five methods rather one.
code: 1967  Bühlmann credibility           experience rating formalised 1970  Bühlmann-Straub                premium-varying credibility                                      (volume-weighted estimator) 1972  Bornhuetter-Ferguson           prior + observed loss blending 1984  Sherman                        chain ladder tail extrapolation 1985  Stanard (Cape Cod)             reserving application of B-S 1993  Mack                           distribution-free MSE for chain                                      ladder

what: The core framework: loss / premium / ratio
title: Projection methods
text: estimation rests three observable quantities: three stochastic observables developing cohort x dev grid. Premium fixed underwriting volume; developing quantity driven morbidity x sum insured x persistency — volume measure Mack (1993), natural weight Bühlmann-Straub (1970). cohort ii development period kk projection methods use notation: Ci,kLC^L_{,k} — cumulative loss Ci,kPC^P_{,k} — cumulative risk premium (premium) fk=Ck+1L/CkLf_k = C^L_{k+1} / C^L_k — age--age (chain ladder) factor gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k — exposure-driven intensity maturity point k*k^* — development period fkf_k stabilises group (detected CV / RSE thresholds) use surgery group throughout brevity — every step generalises multi-group input.
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) tri <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "surgery"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   premium  = "incr_premium" )

title: Projection methods
text: three direct-estimation methods estimate factors data chain forward. order — ed -> cl -> sa — mirrors methodological progression primitive (ED) -> classical (CL) -> composition (SA).

title: Projection methods
text: ED projects every future increment using premium (risk premium) denominator: Ĉ,k+1L=Ĉ,kL+gk⋅Ci,kP \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} = \hat{C}^L_{,k} + g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} ED default unconditional safe baseline: maturity regime detection required, projection robust early-dev age--age volatility. small CkC_k early dev never enters denominator, ED inflate estimate way early fkf_k can. trade-cohort homogeneity. pooled intensity gkg_k assumes cohorts reasonably homogeneous loss-per-premium level. cohort-level drift projection biases toward pooled mean may -project post-change cohorts — see regime argument explicit filtering. use ED: baseline cohort homogeneity plausible; short-tail products chain ladder offers advantage; sparse data age--age factors unreliable across links.

title: Projection methods
text: CL classical Mack (1993) model: Ĉ,k+1L=fk⋅Ĉ,kL \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot \hat{C}^L_{,k} cohort’s cumulative loss acts anchor, cohort-level drift propagates naturally without explicit regime detection. trade-mirror image ED’s: CL volatile early fkf_k noisy, small denominators amplify link errors. Within fit_ratio(), CL method projects loss premium forward — via chain ladder column — computes loss-ratio uncertainty via delta method. loss lane alone equivalent fit_cl(tri). use CL: age--age factors stabilise; cohort-level drift scenarios cohort’s observed trajectory anchor projection; reserving exercises regulators expect classical Mack form documentation.

title: Projection methods
text: SA composes ED maturity point CL , exploiting fact fkf_k volatile early stable late, gkg_k behaves opposite way: Ĉ,k+1L={Ĉ,kL+gk⋅Ci,kPk<k*(ED maturity)fk⋅Ĉ,kLk≥k*(CL maturity) \hat{C}^L_{,k+1} \;=\; \begin{cases} \hat{C}^L_{,k} + g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} & k < k^* \quad \text{(ED maturity)} \\ f_k \cdot \hat{C}^L_{,k}              & k \ge k^* \quad \text{(CL maturity)} \end{cases} maturity SA anchors loss estimate premium volume, avoiding volatile-link explosion classical CL suffers early fkf_k noisy. maturity SA preserves cohort’s observed level, avoiding “cohorts converge average” behaviour pure ED suffers tail. use SA: long-tail portfolios early dev volatile (ED phase) cohort-level drift needs cohort-anchored projection later dev (CL phase); recent cohorts (immature data) mixed older cohorts (matured); health insurance cohorts structural pre-/post-maturity difference (e.g. waiting-period transitions).

title: Projection methods
Projection methods
text: ED, CL, SA estimate factors data. observed triangle thin — immature cohorts, cohorts right rate change — estimate can unstable. prior-anchored family blends expected loss ratio (ELR) whatever loss already emerged: Ult=Llatest+(1−1LDF)⋅π⋅ELR \text{Ult} = L_{\text{latest}}   + \left(1 - \tfrac{1}{\text{LDF}}\right) \cdot \pi \cdot \text{ELR} two methods differ ELR comes .

title: Projection methods
text: ED, CC, BF differ aggregate loss-per-premium signal: ED — per-link gkg_k: dev-granular, cohort-uniform per link. CC — cohort-level single ELR: cohort-uniform, dev-aggregated. BF — external prior: bypasses data estimation altogether. Together five methods reconstruct P&C reserving trinity long-term health, three aggregation axes serve distinct use case.

what: Chain ladder as a reserving worker
title: Projection methods
text: fit_cl() dedicated chain ladder fit single value column. Unlike fit_ratio() — projects loss premium jointly get loss ratio — fit_cl() projects one cumulative metric forward computes Mack-style standard errors per cohort. classical P&C reserving use case: projecting ultimate paid / incurred loss open accident year. Practitioners P&C background recognise Mack workflow directly. fit_cl() summarises adjacent development links age--age factors fk=Ck+1L/CkLf_k = C^L_{k+1} / C^L_k, selected per link chained project cohort forward ultimate. top point projection, Mack’s formulae decompose prediction variance process parameter components: loss_proc_se — process variance, σk2\sigma^2_k (residual link variance per development period). loss_param_se — parameter variance, uncertainty selected age--age factors f̂k\hat{f}_k. loss_total_se — total standard error, loss_proc_se2+loss_param_se2\sqrt{\text{loss\_proc\_se}^2 + \text{loss\_param\_se}^2}. loss_total_cv — coefficient variation, loss_total_se / loss_proj.   plot_triangle() displays cohort x dev cells heatmap, distinguishing observed cells projected, label_style showing per-cell CV / SE / CI:

title: Projection methods
text: fit_ratio() reports analytical standard errors L/P. Two variants control premium uncertainty enters: se_method = "fixed" (default) — premium treated fixed, SE(L/P)≈SE(L)/P\text{SE}(L/P) \approx \text{SE}(L)/P. Strictly delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0. se_method = "delta" — full delta method including premium uncertainty loss-premium correlation rho: Var(L/P)≈Var(L)P2+L2Var(P)P4−2ρLSE(L)SE(P)P3 \mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{P^2}   + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(P)}{P^4}   - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P)}{P^3} two complementary paths SE estimation: Analytical — .mack_f_var() (Mack 1993) .ed_g_var() (B-S 1970) provide closed-form per-link variance, distribution-free. Bootstrap — residual paradigm (cell / link / parametric) drives forward simulation, capturing distributional shape. Residual choice determines process variance scale forward-sim model (paradigm matching). large divergence two paths model-misspecification signal; routinely computing serves sanity check.

title: Projection methods
text: method = "sa" maturity point k*k^* determines projection switches ED CL. default fit_ratio() / fit_loss() use maturity = "auto", calls detect_maturity() internally default thresholds. forms: maturity = NULL — disable detection (meaningful non-SA methods fit_ata() standalone). maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.05, min_run = 2L) — lazy spec forwarding custom thresholds detect_maturity() fit time (leakage-safe backtest()). maturity = detect_maturity(tri, ...) — pre-built Maturity object, fixed across refits. maturity = maturity_at(coverage = "surgery", change = 4) — manual per-group override (e.g. company-standard k*k^*). lazy-spec / pre-built / manual pattern applies fit_cl(), maturity filtering restricts projection mature region selected ATA factors volatile.

title: Projection methods
text: ED default unconditional safe baseline: maturity regime detection required, conservative early-dev volatility. methods warranted ED’s homogeneity assumption unrealistic, external anchor preferable factors estimated thin triangle. practice: start "ed" (default), run "cl" "sa" sensitivity. three agree, projection robust. diverge, inspect maturity detection, regime filters, underlying ATA factors. Reach "bf" / "cc" cohort immature data-method trustworthy.
code: Default is "ed" -- pooled g_k x cohort premium, no maturity dependency.  Move to another method when ED's assumptions become limiting:   |-- Cohort-level drift (regime change, underwriting / coverage shift)   |     -> "cl"  (cohort's own cum_loss anchors the drift)   |-- Long-tail portfolio with BOTH volatile early dev AND cohort drift   |     -> "sa"  (ED before maturity, CL after)   |-- All cohorts already past maturity, no early-dev volatility   |     -> "cl"  (no ED region needed)   |-- Immature / post-rate-change cohorts, an external ELR is on hand   |     -> "bf"  (blend the external prior with emerged loss)   |-- A single coherent ELR target, no external prior available         -> "cc"  (data-pooled ELR, same blending form as BF)

title: lossratio
text: 장기 건강보험 손해율 분석·예측·모니터링 도구. Maturity 기반 단계 적응형(stage-adaptive: ED → CL) 손해율 예측 + Regime change 탐지(인수 정책 변경 등 코호트 축 구조 변화) + backtest 검증을 한 워크플로로 묶는다.   위 한 그림 — 패키지가 무엇을 하는지 한눈에: 🟦 used — 적합에 사용된 셀 🟥 holdout — backtest 검증용 대각셀 ⬜ unused — regime / recent 필터로 제외된 영역 수직 dash선 — maturity k*k^* (ED → CL 전환 경계) 수평 dash선 — regime k**k^{**} (코호트 축 컷)

title: lossratio
text: 장기 건강보험은 코호트 안에서 클레임과 보험료가 오랜 기간 지속적으로 발생하고 신상품 출시·보험료 갱신·인수 정책 등 구조적 변화가 자주 일어난다. 이 사실이 손해율 예측을 어렵게 만든다. 세 component 가 한 figure 에서 동시에 작동하는 것을 위 그림이 보여준다.

title: lossratio
lossratio
code: library(lossratio) data(experience)           # 번들 합성 데이터 (4 coverages)  # 1) Triangle 구축 — long-format 데이터 → 코호트 × dev 구조 exp_surgery <- experience[coverage == "surgery"] tri <- as_triangle(   exp_surgery,   groups    = "coverage",   cohort    = "uy_m",   calendar  = "cy_m",   loss      = "incr_loss",   premium   = "incr_premium",   cell_type = "incremental"   # default; "cumulative" 면 누적 입력 ) plot(tri)  # 2) 손해율 적합 (default: ED — exposure-driven baseline. #    코호트-anchored projection 원할 시 method = "cl" 또는 "sa") ratio <- fit_ratio(tri) summary(ratio) plot_triangle(ratio)  # 3) 환경변화 (regime shift) 자동 탐지 — 인수 정책 변경 시점 detect_regime(tri, by = "coverage", method = "e_divisive")  # 4) backtest — 최근 6 대각선을 빼고 fit + actual 비교 bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L, target = "ratio")  # target = "ratio" / "loss" / "premium" plot(bt) plot_triangle(bt)

title: lossratio
text: fit_ratio(method = "ed") (default) 또는 fit_ed(). 모든 손해 증분이 보험료(위험보험료)를 분모로 사용: ΔCL=gk⋅CkP\Delta C^L = g_k \cdot C^P_k. 무조건적 안전한 baseline – 성숙점 / regime 검출 의존 없음, 초기 dev 의 ATA 인자 변동에 강건. 언제 사용: baseline 으로. pooled intensity gkg_k 는 코호트들이 단위 보험료 당 손해 수준에서 대체로 동질적이라고 가정 – 코호트-레벨 drift(약관 / 인수 정책 변경) 시 pooled 평균으로 편향, post-change 코호트를 -project 가능. 명시적 필터는 regime 인자.

title: lossratio
text: fit_ratio(method = "cl") 또는 fit_cl(). 고전 Mack (1993) 체인 래더 Ck+1L=fk⋅CkLC^L_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C^L_k + 해석적 표준오차. 코호트 자기 cum_loss 가 anchor 역할이므로 코호트-레벨 drift 가 자연스럽게 propagation – 명시적 regime 검출 불필요. 언제 사용: ATA 인자가 안정된 후. 특히 코호트-레벨 drift 시나리오에서 코호트의 관측 경로가 자동 anchor.

title: lossratio
text: fit_ratio(method = "sa"). 두 방법의 합성: 성숙점 이전은 ED, 이후는 CL. ED 의 초기 dev 안정성 + CL 의 후기 dev 코호트 anchoring 결합. 언제 사용: 장기-tail 포트폴리오 – 초기 dev 의 변동성(ED phase) + 후기 dev 의 코호트-레벨 drift(CL phase) 둘 다 처리.

title: lossratio
text: fit_loss(method = "bf") / fit_bf() 와 fit_loss(method = "cc") / fit_cc(). 두 방법 모두 예상 손해율 (ELR) 과 관측 손해를 결합한다: Ult=Llatest+(1−q)⋅ELR⋅Eult\text{Ult} = L_{\text{latest}} + (1 - q) \cdot \text{ELR} \cdot E_{\text{ult}}. Bornhuetter-Ferguson (1972) 는 ELR 을 외부 prior 로 받고, Cape Cod (Stanard 1985) 는 payout 가중으로 데이터에서 추정한다. 언제 사용: 미성숙 코호트 또는 요율 변경 직후 코호트 – 관측 데이터만으로 예측을 anchoring 하기에 너무 빈약할 때. 신뢰할 외부 prior 가 있으면 BF, 포트폴리오가 단일 코호트 응집 ELR 목표를 시사하면 CC.

title: lossratio
text: long-format data.frame / data.table. 컬럼명은 자유 — as_triangle() 인자로 어떤 이름이든 넘기면 함수가 표준화함. 해석을 정하는 두 인자: cell_type — "incremental" (default) 또는 "cumulative". 일반적 raw experience 는 incremental; 이미 누적 합산된 데이터라면 cell_type = "cumulative" 로 넘기면 함수가 per-cohort diff 로 incremental 도 derive. grain — "auto" (default, cohort 날짜로부터 자동 감지) 또는 "M" / "Q" / "H" / "Y". 월별 / 분기별 / 반기별 / 연간 집계로 bin. as_triangle() 가 스키마 검증 + 날짜 coerce + (calendar / dev 중 하나만 줬을 때) 나머지 축 derive + grain bin + cumulative / incremental 컬럼 + ratio / margin / profit 까지 한 번에 생성.

title: lossratio
text: plot_triangle() 의 두 인자가 직교: region — 어느 영역? "proj" / "full" / "data" view — 무엇을 보여줄지? "value" (metric) / "usage" (cell status)
code: plot(tri)                                                  # 코호트 궤적 plot_triangle(tri)                                         # cell heatmap plot_triangle(ratio, view = "value", region = "proj")      # 예측 영역 손해율 plot_triangle(ratio, view = "usage")                       # 맨 위 이미지와 동일 모드

what: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
text: Validate raw experience data, aggregate along single calendar-period axis, assign Calendar S3 class associated plot.Calendar() / summary.Calendar() / longer-form methods dispatch result. Compared as_triangle(), builds two-dimensional cohort x dev structure, as_calendar() one-dimensional: single calendar-period time series (per group) showing portfolio evolves time, regardless cohort membership. result long-format data.table class c("Calendar", "data.table", "data.frame") containing cumulative loss / premium, incremental cumulative LR, margin, profit, share columns within calendar cell. cumulative loss ratio defined : $$ratio = loss / premium$$ long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used premium measure. Proportion variables computed within calendar cell: incr_loss_share    = incr_loss    / sum(incr_loss) incr_premium_share = incr_premium / sum(incr_premium) loss_share         = loss         / sum(loss) premium_share      = premium      / sum(premium) Therefore, fixed calendar cell, proportions sum 1 across groups. useful examining composition calendar period across products grouping variables. Calendar derives calendar = cohort + (dev - 1) using Triangle's grain attribute aggregates incremental loss / premium columns (groups, calendar). works Triangles built either mode (without original calendar column raw experience), since cohort + dev always sufficient reconstruct calendar axis Triangle's grain.

title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
text: data.frame class "Calendar", containing following derived columns: cal_idx Sequential calendar-period index within group (1, 2, ..., N). Time-series convention; intentionally dev – Calendar integer just rank date within group, true development period (cym - uym). what: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
text: Derive development-link table Triangle assign Link S3 class associated summary.Link(), plot.Link(), fit_ata(), fit_intensity(), etc. methods dispatch result. Unlike as_triangle() / as_calendar() / as_total() (take raw experience data validate/aggregate ), as_link() operates Triangle (already validated upstream) reshapes link-pair rows. row corresponds one development link (cohort, ata_from -> ata_to), long-format intermediate underlying chain ladder (CL) exposure-driven (ED) workflows. Two modes produced depending exposure: Single-variable mode (exposure = NULL) age--age factor \(ata = value_{} / value_{}\), \(value\) column named loss. Dual-variable mode (exposure supplied) addition loss-side ATA, exposure-driven intensity \(g = \Delta loss / premium_{}\) computed stored intensity column. exposure measure denominator loss ratio calculations; long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used.

title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link

title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
text: x Triangle object. loss single cumulative metric used link numerator. Must one "loss", "premium", "ratio". Default "loss". loss-side ATA cumulative loss column, cumulative metric Triangle may supplied. exposure Optional second cumulative metric, treated exposure base (denominator anchor) ED workflow. Must one "loss", "premium", "ratio", must differ loss. NULL (default), single-variable columns produced. weight Optional cumulative metric used WLS weight downstream summary / fit_ata calls. Must differ loss. combined exposure (dual workflow anchor). min_denom Minimum denominator required compute ata intensity. loss_from <= min_denom, ata becomes NA; premium_from <= min_denom, intensity becomes NA. Default 0. drop_invalid Logical; TRUE, rows non-finite ata (single-var) non-finite intensity (dual-var) dropped. Default FALSE full link grid preserved diagnostics.

what: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
text: Validate raw experience data, aggregate onto (group, cohort, dev) grid, assign Triangle S3 class downstream methods (fit_ratio(), fit_loss(), backtest(), plot(), plot_triangle(), detect_maturity(), detect_regime(), detect_convergence(), ...) can dispatch result. Three steps happen inside single call: Validate – required columns present, dates coerce cleanly, grain consistent. Hard errors schema issues downstream code never receives malformed input. Standardise + aggregate – rename package-canonical column names (cohort, calendar, dev, loss, premium, ...), auto-detect grain (M / Q / H / Y) cohort spacing, derive dev (cohort, calendar), aggregate (group, cohort, dev), enrich cumulative / share / LR columns. Tag – set S3 class c("Triangle", "data.table", "data.frame") every *.Triangle method becomes available. lossratio's Triangle data.table long format (one row per (group, cohort, dev) cell) enriched columns described . name Triangle refers conceptual cohort x dev triangular region – older cohorts observed dev cells newer ones – matrix layout. auto-grain detection (grain = "auto", default) reads cohort value spacing; explicit values must least coarse input grain. user pre-bin data supply dev_* column. result contains: cumulative loss cumulative premium, per-period cumulative proportions, per-period cumulative margin, profit indicators, per-period loss ratio (incr_ratio = incr_loss / incr_premium) cumulative loss ratio (ratio = loss / premium). cumulative loss ratio defined : $$ratio = loss / premium$$ long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used premium measure. Proportion variables computed within (cohort, dev) cell: incr_loss_share    = incr_loss    / sum(incr_loss) incr_premium_share = incr_premium / sum(incr_premium) loss_share         = loss         / sum(loss) premium_share      = premium      / sum(premium) Therefore, fixed (cohort, dev) cell, proportions sum 1 across groups. useful examining composition development cell across products grouping variables.

title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
text: df data.frame containing experience data per-period loss premium columns plus cohort calendar Date columns (input internal Date coercion accepts: Date, POSIXt, integer yyyy / yyyymm / yyyymmdd, ISO string). groups Column(s) used grouping (e.g., product, gender). cohort Single column (raw name) defining underwriting / premium period start (e.g., "uy_m"). calendar Single column (raw name) defining calendar period observation (e.g., "cy_m"). Optional – supply either calendar dev (). calendar given, dev derived internally via count_periods(cohort, calendar, grain). dev Single column (raw name) holding pre-computed development periods (e.g., "dev_m"). Optional – supply either calendar dev (). dev given, calendar axis omitted attribute (downstream calendar-diagonal logic uses cohort + dev). given, dev cross-checked count_periods(cohort, calendar, grain). loss Single character; per-period loss column df (raw name, e.g., "incr_loss"). premium Single character; per-period premium column df (raw name, e.g., "incr_premium"). Premium measure used denominator loss ratio calculations. long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used. grain One "auto" (default), "M", "Q", "H", "Y". "auto" infers grain cohort value spacing. Explicit values must least coarse input grain; input binned (floored) grain aggregation. cell_type One "incremental" (default) "cumulative". Whether loss premium df already hold per-period (incremental) values cumulative-within-cohort values. internal triangle always built incremental representation; "cumulative" inputs differenced first. fill_gaps Logical; TRUE, zero-fill missing (groups, cohort, dev) cells every cohort consecutive dev sequence. Default FALSE, raises error gaps detected. Use validate_triangle() inspect gaps deciding.

title: Backtest a loss / premium / loss-ratio projection on existing data — backtest

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
text: Generate B alternative realizations Triangle via nonparametric (England-Verrall residual) parametric (Mack normal closed-form) Stage 1 perturbation. output model-agnostic BootstrapTriangle object downstream fit functions (fit_cl / fit_ed / fit_ratio) consume recover parameter process risk decomposition. entry point sits Triangle level – knows nothing CL, ED, SA. fit method later refits model every alt triangle adds Stage 2 process noise using variance recipe. bootstrap object therefore reusable across fit methods. Bootstrap proceeds two conceptual stages (see dev/BOOTSTRAP.md): Stage 1 – parameter uncertainty: residual resample (parametric Normal draw) propagated cumulative loss chain, refitted factors per replicate. produces B alternative mean predictions per cell. Stage 2 – process uncertainty: added inside fit function demand, using method-specific sigma^2. process argument stored metadata consuming fit method knows distribution use.

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
code: bootstrap(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Triangle' bootstrap(   x,   type = c("parametric", "nonparametric", "analytical"),   residual = c("cell", "link"),   hat_adj = TRUE,   demean = TRUE,   process = c("gamma", "od_pois", "normal", "lognormal"),   method = c("ed", "cl", "sa"),   pooling = c("pooled", "separated", "tail_pooled"),   tail = c("auto", "maturity"),   min_pool = 5L,   maturity = NULL,   target = c("loss", "premium"),   B = 499L,   seed = NULL,   alpha = 1,   quantile_ci = FALSE,   keep_pseudo = FALSE,   ... )

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
text: x Triangle object. ... Reserved future use. type One "parametric" (default), "nonparametric", "analytical". "analytical" draws new link factors N(f_hat, sqrt(Var(f_hat))) (Mack 1993 closed-form propagation; CL ). "nonparametric" resamples standardized residuals reconstructs pseudo triangle (England-Verrall / Pinheiro). "parametric" draws active cell directly ProcessDist(mu_hat, phi) refits synthetic triangle (textbook England-Verrall 1999 parametric bootstrap; supports three methods cl / ed / sa). type left unset, function picks type best matches method: cl defaults "analytical" (fastest), ed / sa default "parametric" (cleanest additive / stage-adaptive variance decomposition). residual Residual scope type = "nonparametric". One "cell" (default – England-Verrall 1999/2002, Pearson residuals incremental cells; ODP GLM equivalent via Renshaw-Verrall 1998) "link" (Mack 1993 / Pinheiro 2003, Pearson residuals link factors). hat_adj Logical. Hat-matrix leverage adjustment (r_h = r / sqrt(1 - h_ii)) cell residual path. Default TRUE (England-Verrall 2002 Addendum); set FALSE use simpler degrees--freedom factor sqrt(n / (n - p)) (England-Verrall 1999). defined residual = "cell"; warned-ignored otherwise. Note hat_adj drops corner cells h_ii = 1 (always (, 1) (1, J) upper triangle); small triangles can meaningful pool reduction – set FALSE matters. demean Logical. Subtract per-group residual mean residual cell-residual pool resampling (de- + mean: remove mean, leaving zero-mean pool). Default TRUE. Shapland (2010, Sec.4.2) discusses one option among others – see Bootstrap SE decomposition vignette trade-. defined residual = "cell"; warned-ignored otherwise. process One "gamma", "od_pois", "normal", "lognormal". Stored metadata; downstream fit functions read choose Stage 2 noise distribution. "gamma" default non-negative right-skewed loss data. "lognormal" reserved Phase 5b.3 currently errors. method Fit-model paradigm whose lower-triangle forward projection bootstrap produce. One "ed" (default – exposure-driven additive recursion across dev; Phase 1 keeps premium fixed, projected via CL), "cl" (chain-ladder multiplicative recursion across dev), "sa" (stage-adaptive – ED maturity, CL ; currently routes CL kernel pending Phase 4 SA bootstrap). Mirrors method argument fit_loss(); resulting BootstrapTriangle consumed corresponding fit_*(..., bootstrap = bt) branch. "ed" requires residual = "cell"; ED + residual = "link" implemented. pooling Residual-pool grouping. One "pooled", "separated", "tail_pooled". "pooled" shares residuals across links; "separated" keeps development link independent (Mack-faithful); "tail_pooled" uses per-link pools cut single pooled bucket . tail Tail-cut rule pooling = "tail_pooled". One "auto" (cut smallest ata_to whose residual count drops min_pool) "maturity" (cut resolved Maturity change point). min_pool Minimum residual count per per-link pool pooling = "tail_pooled" && tail = "auto". Default 5. maturity Required pooling = "tail_pooled" && tail = "maturity". Four-type dispatch: NULL, Maturity object, string "auto", function function(tri) -> Maturity. target Cumulative metric perturb. One "loss" (default) "premium". value column $pseudo_triangles named target downstream refit helpers know column read. B Number bootstrap replicates. Default 499 – Davison & Hinkley (1997) convention picks B (B + 1) p integer target quantile p. B = 499 (B + 1 = 500), every one-sided VaR commonly reported reserving – p = 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99 – lands exact integer ordinal index, empirical CI exact without interpolation. Solvency II / K-ICS 99.5% TVaR reporting pass B = 1999; gold-standard published convention pass B = 999. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. alpha Variance exponent Mack's Var(C_{k+1} | C_k) = sigma_k^2 C_k^alpha. Default 1 (volume-weighted). quantile_ci Logical. Opt-flag empirical percentile CI columns (ci_lo / ci_hi = 2.5% / 97.5% quantiles loss_sampled across replicates, Davison & Hinkley (1997) type=1 ordinal) $summary slot. Default FALSE. Normal- approximation CI (mean_proj +/- 1.96 * total_se) derivable total_se usually enough interactive use; set TRUE Solvency II / K-ICS VaR reporting need empirical tail. C kernel computes CI bounds pass SE decomposition (per-cell qsort dominated Stage 1 work), marginal cost FALSE small. keep_pseudo Logical. Whether materialise per-replicate long-format pseudo_triangles slot. Default FALSE (changed TRUE v0.x performance). TRUE builds long-format data.table diagnostic inspection (e.g. raw replicate trajectories, custom quantile work). typical 4-group monthly triangle B = 999 reshape costs ~250-300 ms ~200 MB top $summary; users consume $summary (common case) leave FALSE. fit_ratio() / fit_loss() / fit_premium() always pass FALSE internally read $summary. Set TRUE explicitly want inspect $pseudo_triangles directly.

title: Bootstrap a Triangle — bootstrap
text: object class BootstrapTriangle (list) elements: pseudo_triangles Long-format data.table columns [groups], cohort, dev, rep, loss. rep ranges 1..B. Observed-region cells contain residual-perturbed (original "analytical") cumulative loss; missing region contains Stage 1 forward projection means. residual_pool data.table standardized residuals used, pool_id column identifying pool residual belongs (depends method/tail). Schema differs residual mode: [groups, cohort, ata_from, ata_to, residual, pool_id] residual = "link", [groups, cohort, dev, residual, pool_id] residual = "cell". f_anchor Per-link Mack factor estimates f_hat n_cohorts. sigma2_anchor Per-link Mack sigma^2 Var(f_hat). meta list(type, residual, hat_adj, process, method, pooling, tail, min_pool, B, seed, alpha, target, groups, maturity).

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(experience) tri <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "surgery"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "incr_loss",   premium = "incr_premium" )  # Cell-residual bootstrap (default) boots <- bootstrap(tri, type = "nonparametric", residual = "cell",                    hat_adj = TRUE, process = "gamma",                    B = 500, seed = 1) print(boots)  # Link-residual bootstrap (Mack 1993 / Pinheiro 2003 path) boots_link <- bootstrap(tri, type = "nonparametric", residual = "link",                         pooling = "separated", process = "gamma",                         B = 500, seed = 1) } # }

what: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: Identify first dev \(k^{**}\) projected portfolio loss ratio observed stable maximum available development period \(V\). Three complementary stability criteria computed LR backtest path; user selects one defines \(k^{**}\) via method =. Notation mapping (code <-> math): Standard chain-ladder convention: \(\) indexes cohort (origin period), \(k\) indexes development period. maturity point \(k^*\) convergence point \(k^{**}\) live \(k\) axis. Earlier paper drafts used \(v\) (valuation) index Section 11; unify \(k\) consistency. Stability methods (sequence drives pass): "window" Local stability: drift_window[] < max_drift. Fast, misses slow monotone drift fits max_drift per step. "tail" (default, reserving-safe) Global stability: drift_tail[] < max_drift. Catches monotone drift. first passing dev later (conservative) "window". "slope" Trend test: |slope[]| < max_slope. Explicit -trend check; sensitive non-linear trajectories. "" Strictest: three pass simultaneously. four pass vectors (pass_window, pass_tail, pass_slope, pass) underlying diagnostic series returned regardless chosen method, analyst can inspect every criterion re-decide. Across methods, cross-cohort agreement clause dispersion[] < max_dispersion required addition. replaces earlier formulation \(R_k < c \cdot \hat{SE}^{param}_k\) (paper Section 11). paper's SE-normalised form asymptotically broken large portfolios: \(\hat{SE}^{param}\) shrinks \(1/\sqrt{n}\) \(R_k\) structural noise floor, ratio diverges criterion never fires. Caveat (reserving): detected conv_k reflects stability dev_max (\(K_{\max}\)) – asymptotic guarantee projection drift past \(K_{\max}\). Treat conv_k diagnostic ", observe stable", guarantee future stability. reserving applications, prefer method = "tail" "" "window", attach IBNR margin via fit_ratio$summary SE/CI columns, weigh evidence span (dev_max - conv_k): conv_k near dev_max thin evidence.

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence

Used method = "slope" / "". max_dispersion Upper bound cross-cohort dispersion \(\hat{D}_k\). Default 0.15. window Drift window length \(W\) (dev steps): number consecutive valuations used "window" method compute drift_window. Default 5L. what: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: Identify first mature age--age (ata) link Triangle. Internally builds single-variable Link table, computes per-link diagnostic via summary.Link() model = "ata", locates first link whose statistics satisfy maturity criteria. Maturity determined using combination : cv < max_cv rse < max_rse valid_ratio >= min_valid_ratio n_valid >= min_n_valid optional consecutive maturity min_run ata links cv rse must satisfied simultaneously. cv captures raw variability observed ata factors across cohorts, rse reflects precision WLS-estimated factor. Using criteria together provides robust maturity assessment either alone. Default loss = "loss" (cumulative loss). Maturity chain ladder methodologically property loss development: ATA factors cumulative loss stabilize chain ladder becomes reliable, turn makes downstream LR projection reliable. ATA factors ratio (ratio two cumulative quantities) carry additional noise tend give less precise maturity decisions. Override loss specifically want maturity premium development another cumulative metric.

what: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: Detect structural change points sequence cohort-level development trajectories. underwriting cohort (indexed cohort "Triangle" object) treated feature vector whose entries selected loss metric observed development periods 1, ..., window. Cohorts ordered underwriting period tested structural shifts multivariate sequence. Multi-group Triangle inputs supported: detection runs independently per group, results combined single Regime object whose $changes, $labels, etc. carry group column. Single-group input retains original scalar / Date-vector / matrix layout backward compatibility. Two detection strategies supported: "e_divisive" Multivariate non-parametric divisive change-point detection energy statistic (Matteson & James 2014). number regimes determined data; changes significant sig_level retained. Preferred number regimes known advance. "hclust" Ward hierarchical clustering scaled cohort feature matrix, cut n_regimes clusters. Ignores time ordering – useful sanity check since non-adjacent cohorts may cluster together trajectory pattern strictly chronological.

text: x object class "Triangle". May contain one groups (per-group detection runs independently). Also used S3 print() method Regime objects. loss Trajectory variable. Default "ratio" (cumulative loss ratio). Accepts column Triangle (e.g. "ratio", "loss", "premium", "incr_loss", "incr_premium"), plus three diagnostic derived metrics computed inline per (group, cohort): "loss_ata" Loss age--age factor loss[k+1] / loss[k] – multiplicative loss development speed (CL $f_k$). "premium_ata" Premium age--age factor – form premium. "loss_ed" Loss intensity (loss[k] - loss[k-1]) / premium[k-1] – additive, exposure-anchored (ED model's $g_k$). "premium_ed" Alias "premium_ata" – two differ constant (premium_ata - 1), PCA standardization detection removes shift, yield identical regime changes. Provided API symmetry loss_ata / loss_ed pair. Derived metrics drop first dev row per cohort (predecessor), re-index dev detection sees contiguous sequence. See vignette("diagnostics") "Choice loss" section guidance loss metric matches suspected event. Grouping column(s) per-combination detection. NULL (default) reuses Triangle's attr(x, "groups") non-empty – detect_regime(tri) dispatches per group automatically – otherwise falls back pooled detection. Pass = character(0) force pooled detection multi-group Triangle, character vector (subset names(x)) dispatch explicit combo, e.g. = "coverage" = c("channel", "coverage"). window Trajectory window. Integer (e.g., 12L) fixed window, string "auto" (default) – resolves group's maturity via detect_maturity(), falling back 6L maturity unavailable (NA, pooled mode, mismatching Triangle's attr("groups")). Cohorts fewer resolved window observed periods dropped. method One "e_divisive", "hclust". n_regimes Integer. Number regimes force. NULL means auto-detect "e_divisive"; ignored (required equal requested value) "hclust", default 2. sig_level Significance level "e_divisive". Default 0.05. min_size Minimum segment size "e_divisive". Default 3. treatment downstream fits apply Regime $changes contains multiple change points. modes mask triangle bridged development band: segment's natural mini-triangle wall (dev >= max_cal - seg_last + 1) widened calendar-diagonal bridge anchored next (newer) segment's first-cohort midpoint dev. bridge closes factor gaps otherwise open segment boundaries, band carries continuous run age--age factors f_1, ..., f_(K-1) every cohort can projected full development length K. One : "segment_bridged" (default) Pool whole bridged band single factor estimate – every cohort dev k uses f_k, estimated whichever cohorts reach dev inside band (recent ones, construction). per-segment fit: development pattern treated shared across regimes, band's lower boundary regime-aware. "segment_bridged_borrowed" Estimate factors per segment (early-dev factors stay regime-specific), borrow late-dev factors segment estimate another segment can (bridge guarantees donor exists). cohort projects segment's factors available borrowed factors beyond reach. ... Reserved future use. object object class "Regime". Used S3 summary() method.

what: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor
title: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor
text: Internal helper used amount_divisor = "auto". Considers SI/financial prefix set {1, 1e3, 1e6, 1e9, 1e12} (unit / thousand / million / billion / trillion) picks largest divisor median / divisor still rounds (%.1f) non-zero label. 0.05 threshold label format "0.0" precision wiped , divisors disqualified. Falls back 1 data finite positive values. "largest divisor still rounds non-zero" rule favours labels 0.X range (3 chars) X.X / XX.X / XXX.X (3-5 chars). keeps -cell heatmap text compact; colour fill carries precise value. Examples (median -> divisor, label): 5e2  -> 1e3,  "0.5" 5e7  -> 1e9,  "0.1" (since 5e7 / 1e9 = 0.05 >= 0.05) 5e10 -> 1e12, "0.1" 5e12 -> 1e12, "5.0" (capped largest candidate) numeric >= 0.05 test deliberate: Windows R's sprintf("%.1f", 0.05) rounds "0.0" Linux / macOS round "0.1". Comparing numerically (tiny 1e-10 slack) produces deterministic, platform-independent choice.

what: Analytical BF / Cape Cod prediction error (Mack 2008 decomposition) — .bf_analytical_se
title: Analytical BF / Cape Cod prediction error (Mack 2008 decomposition) — .bf_analytical_se
text: Closed-form mean squared error prediction per-cohort BF / Cape Cod ultimate, following decomposition Mack (2008, "Prediction Error Bornhuetter/Ferguson", ASTIN Bulletin 38(1), Section 5): $$\mathrm{msep}(R_i) = \mathrm{proc}_i +   (\hat U_i^2 + \mathrm{Var}(\hat U_i))\,\mathrm{Var}(q_i) +   \mathrm{Var}(\hat U_i)\,(1 - q_i)^2$$ three terms process error, development-pattern estimation error, prior estimation error. point estimate unchanged – variance added ("framework borrowed" approach: Mack's three-term structure variances sourced lossratio's fits). Variance inputs: \(\mathrm{Var}(\hat U_i)\) – prior ultimate \(\hat U_i = \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E^{ult}_i\) product two independent factors, \(\mathrm{Var}(\hat U_i) = (E^{ult}_i)^2\,\mathrm{Var}(\mathrm{ELR}_i)     + \mathrm{ELR}_i^2\,\mathrm{Var}(E^{ult}_i)     + \mathrm{Var}(\mathrm{ELR}_i)\,\mathrm{Var}(E^{ult}_i)\). Var(ELR) comes distribution prior's elr_se (0 deterministic prior); Var(E_ult) premium fit SE. \(\mathrm{Var}(q_i)\) – delta method \(q_i = L^{obs}_i / L^{ult,CL}_i\), using CL parameter SE. process – CL process variance scaled BF / CL reserve ratio (process noise taken proportional projected future-loss volume).

title: Analytical BF / Cape Cod prediction error (Mack 2008 decomposition) — .bf_analytical_se
Analytical BF / Cape Cod prediction error (Mack 2008 decomposition) — .bf_analytical_se
text: per_cohort data.table one row per cohort carrying by_cols, q, loss_ult (BF / CC ultimate), loss_latest, reserve, elr, premium_ult, var_elr, var_eult, loss_ult_cl, loss_proc_se, loss_param_se. by_cols c(groups, "cohort"). conf_level Confidence level normal CI bounds.

title: Analytical BF / Cape Cod prediction error (Mack 2008 decomposition) — .bf_analytical_se
text: data.table columns by_cols + c("loss_total_se", "loss_total_cv", "loss_ci_lo", "loss_ci_hi").

what: Per-replicate BF / Cape Cod composition from two BootstrapTriangle — .bf_compose_bootstrap
title: Per-replicate BF / Cape Cod composition from two BootstrapTriangle — .bf_compose_bootstrap
text: Given paired loss-side premium-side BootstrapTriangle objects (keep_pseudo = TRUE per-replicate cohort--dev cum loss / cum premium means available), compose BF / Cape Cod ultimate distribution per replicate: replicate \(b\), derive \(q_i^b =     L_{obs,} / L_{ult,}^{CL,b}\) loss-side Stage 1 mean trajectory (last-dev cell). Derive \(E_i^{ult,b}\) premium-side Stage 1 mean last-dev cell. BF: \(L_{ult,}^{b} = L_{obs,} +     (1 - q_i^b) \cdot \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E_i^{ult,b}\). Cape Cod: per group \(\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC,b} =     \sum_i L_{obs,} / \sum_i E_i^{ult,b} \cdot q_i^b\), plug BF formula. Cell-level projection per replicate: scale per-replicate CL emergence pattern land \(L_{ult,}^{b}\) last dev. Cell-level cohort-level SE / CI SD / quantiles across replicates.

title: Loss ratio SE: ratio_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_ratio_se
text: Ratio-specific internal helper. Two variants: "fixed" (default) Premium treated fixed (non-random). \(\mathrm{SE}(L/P) = \mathrm{SE}(L) / P\). Strictly degenerate case delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0. "delta" First-order Taylor (delta method) including premium uncertainty loss-premium correlation rho: \(\mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx (\mathrm{SE}(L)/P)^2 +       (L \cdot \mathrm{SE}(P) / P^2)^2 -       2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P) / P^3\). variance clipped zero square root (high rho can drive linearised estimate negative). exported; called fit_ratio(). "fixed" branch encodes actuarial assumption earned premium known (estimated), helper generic ratio-SE utility.

title: Loss ratio SE: ratio_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_ratio_se
code: .compute_ratio_se(   loss,   premium,   loss_se,   premium_se = NULL,   method = c("fixed", "delta"),   rho = 0.95 )

title: Loss ratio SE: ratio_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_ratio_se
text: loss Ultimate loss vector (L). premium Ultimate premium vector (E). loss_se SE(L). premium_se SE(P). Unused "fixed"; may NULL. method One "fixed" (default) "delta". rho Loss-premium correlation (-1, 1). Used "delta". Default 0.95.

title: Loss ratio SE: ratio_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_ratio_se

what: Per-cell effective dev_min for the segment mini-triangle band — .compute_segment_mini_tri_bounds
title: Per-cell effective dev_min for the segment mini-triangle band — .compute_segment_mini_tri_bounds
text: cell, returns minimum dev keeps inside segment's fit mask. mask union two regions: segment's natural mini-triangle: dev >= max_cal - seg_last + 1. bridge extension along calendar diagonal anchored next (newer) segment's first-cohort midpoint dev. bridge lets older segment connect successor, filling late-dev cells early cohorts otherwise cut natural mini-triangle wall leave projection cells unreachable. Bridge construction (segments ordered segment_id, lower id = older cohorts). segment s except newest (successor), find segment s+1's first_rank  – cohort rank s+1's first cohort, seg_dev_min – max_cal - last_rank(s+1) + 1, first_cohort_dev_max – max_cal - first_rank(s+1) + 1, mid_dev     – floor((seg_dev_min + first_cohort_dev_max) / 2). bridge diagonal segment s ext_cal_idx(s) = first_rank(s+1) + mid_dev(s+1) - 2 (cell one cohort earlier s+1's first cohort, dev first cohort's mini-triangle midpoint). cell segment s takes effective_dev_min = min(seg_dev_min(s), ext_cal_idx(s) - coh_rank + 1) pmin(..., na.rm = TRUE) natural mini-tri wall stays put bridge applies (last segment, cells whose bridge ray lies wall). Bridges cascade: segment s bridged segment s+1, s+2. bridge ever widens segment's mini-triangle.

title: Per-cell effective dev_min for the segment mini-triangle band — .compute_segment_mini_tri_bounds
Per-cell effective dev_min for the segment mini-triangle band — .compute_segment_mini_tri_bounds
text: coh_ranks Integer vector. Per-cell cohort rank within group. seg_ids Integer vector. Per-cell segment id (1 = oldest). max_cal Integer scalar. Maximum calendar index group. bridge Logical. TRUE (used segment treatments), widen older segment's mini-triangle calendar-diagonal bridge anchored next segment's first-cohort midpoint dev. FALSE, return natural mini-triangle wall (boundary-gap closure); retained diagnostics tests.

title: Per-cell effective dev_min for the segment mini-triangle band — .compute_segment_mini_tri_bounds
