title: Buehlmann-Straub credibility weight per cohort — .credibility_bs
text: Compute per-cohort Buehlmann-Straub credibility factor $$Z_i = \frac{K}{K + s_i^2}$$ replaces emergence fraction \(q_i\) BF / CC blend weight. \(s_i^2\) variance cohort \(\)'s chain ladder loss-ratio estimate, \(K\) variance hypothetical means (VHM) – genuine -cohort spread true loss ratios. standard credibility form \(Z = \tau^2 / (\tau^2 + \sigma^2/w)\) written directly terms per-cohort estimate variance. classical weight q measures much emerged; rare-event green cohort can high q yet chain ladder estimate built almost data. \(s_i^2\) large, \(Z_i \0\) cohort pulled toward prior – exactly protection credibility blend meant give.

title: E-Divisive change-point detection (Matteson & James 2014) — .e_divisive
text: Nonparametric multivariate change-point detection via energy statistic. greedy divisive procedure: within every open segment find split maximises energy distance, accept globally largest candidate permutation test clears sig_level (, k supplied, accept k largest unconditionally), recurse two new segments.

NULL (default) lets permutation test decide many keep. sig_level Permutation-test significance threshold; used k NULL. min_size Minimum size either side split. R Number permutations per significance test. alpha Distance exponent; 1 gives plain Euclidean distance.

(2014), "nonparametric approach multiple change point analysis multivariate data", JASA 109(505), 334-345. greedy recursion per-break permutation test run native kernel C_e_divisive (src/e_divisive.c) – thin wrapper. Permutations draw R's RNG, set.seed() controls reproducibility.

what: Compute ED intensity variance for each development link — .ed_g_var
title: Compute ED intensity variance for each development link — .ed_g_var
text: Internal helper computing \(\mathrm{Var}(\hat{g}_k) = \sigma^2_k / W_k\) \(W_k = \sum_i (C^P_{,k})^{2 - \alpha}\). Buehlmann-Straub (1970) volume-weighted variance applied ED intensity \(g_k = \sum_i \Delta L_{,k} / \sum_i P_{,k-1}\). Paradigm pairing: package keeps two natural analytical variance helpers, one per paradigm-target pair: .mack_f_var() (CL / Mack 1993 applied f-factor) .ed_g_var() (ED / Buehlmann-Straub 1970 applied g-intensity). cross-paradigm pairs (.mack_g_var, .bs_f_var) algebraically derivable via \(g_k = f_k - 1\) (therefore \(\sigma^2_g = \sigma^2_f\)), intentionally provided separate functions avoid suggesting paradigm mismatch encouraged user code. Conceptually factor-level helper (operates per-link $link $selected slots), parallel .mack_f_var(ata_fit: ATAFit). Accepts either "IntensityFit" (factor-level diagnostic ED, sibling "ATAFit") "EDFit" (projection-level, exposes factor-level slots superset). "IntensityFit" path conceptually clean entry point factor-level callers; "EDFit" accepted projection-level callers already hold fit object. Used fit_ed() method = "mack" fit_ratio() ED component.

text: x object class "IntensityFit" "EDFit". Either exposes $link $selected slots used . alpha Numeric scalar. Default 1.

what: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per age--age link: $$C_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot C_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(w_{,k} / C_{,k}^{2 - \alpha}\), \(w_{,k}\) either constant column supplied via weights. corresponds Mack's variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(C_{,k+1} \mid C_{,k}) \propto C_{,k}^{\alpha}\). one observation available link, factor computed directly loss_to / loss_from standard errors set NA. Near-zero values f_se sigma (tol) set zero avoid numerical noise essentially perfect fits.

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
text: x object class "Link". weights Either length-one numeric scalar (default 1) single column name present Link data provides per-row weights. alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure. Default 1. na_rm Logical; TRUE (default), rows non-finite non-positive loss_from dropped fitting. Note loss_to = 0 permitted, zero cumulative values valid observations (e.g. claims yet developed early development periods). tol Non-negative numeric scalar. Values tol set zero. Default 1e-12.

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_ata
text: data.table one row per ata link containing f, f_se, sigma, rse, n_cohorts. rse defined \(f\_se / f\) represents relative standard error WLS-estimated factor. rse NA f_se NA (single observation links) f zero.

what: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed
title: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per development link: $$\Delta C^L_{,k+1} = g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(1 / (C^P_{,k})^{2 - \alpha}\), corresponding variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(\Delta C^L_{,k+1}) \propto (C^P_{,k})^{\alpha}\).

title: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed

what: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
title: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
text: Internal helper computing: $$\mathrm{Var}(\hat{f}_k) = \frac{\sigma^2_k}{W_k}$$ \(W_k = \sum_i w_{,k} \cdot C_{,k}^\alpha\). consistent WLS weight \(w_{,k} / C_{,k}^{2-\alpha}\) used .lm_ata() follows Mack (1993)'s distribution-free standard-error derivation chain ladder reserve estimator. Paradigm pairing: package keeps two natural analytical variance helpers, one per paradigm-target pair: .mack_f_var() (CL / Mack 1993 applied f-factor) .ed_g_var() (ED / Buehlmann-Straub 1970 applied g-intensity). share underlying volume-weighted variance idea (\(\sigma^2_g = \sigma^2_f\) via \(g_k = f_k - 1\)), operate different Link columns (f reads loss_to/loss_from; g reads loss_delta/premium_from) produce differently-named output columns (f_var / g_var), kept separate helpers. Also used fit_ratio() CL component.

title: Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
Compute Mack's factor variance for each development link — .mack_f_var
text: Mack, T. (1993). Distribution-free calculation standard error chain ladder reserve estimates. ASTIN Bulletin, 23(2), 213-225.

what: Compute Mack parameter variance for a single cohort — .mack_param_var
title: Compute Mack parameter variance for a single cohort — .mack_param_var
text: Internal helper computing: $$   \mathrm{param}_{,k+1} =     f_k^2 \cdot \mathrm{param}_{,k} +     \hat{C}_{,k}^2 \cdot \mathrm{Var}(\hat{f}_k) $$

title: Compute Mack parameter variance for a single cohort — .mack_param_var

what: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: Compute group-wise summary statistics age--age factors object class "Link". helper backs summary.Link() dispatcher model = "ata". serves two purposes: Diagnostics: provides descriptive statistics (mean, median, wt, cv) help user assess stability consistency observed ata factors across cohorts. Estimation: fits -intercept weighted least squares model per ata link produce WLS-estimated factor (f), standard error (f_se), relative standard error (rse), Mack sigma (sigma). used downstream detect_maturity() fit_ata().

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: object object class "Link", typically produced as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure WLS fit. Default 1. digits Number decimal places round numeric columns. Default 3. Pass NULL skip rounding. ... Additional arguments passed internal WLS estimation.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: data.table class "ATASummary" containing one row per ata link: ata_from, ata_to, ata_link Link identifiers. mean Arithmetic mean observed ata factors. median Median observed ata factors. wt Volume-weighted mean: \(\sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\), independent alpha. cv Coefficient variation observed ata factors (\(SD / mean\)). Used detect_maturity() assess stability. f WLS-estimated factor. Equals wt alpha = 2 zero loss_from rows present. f_se Standard error WLS-estimated factor. rse Relative standard error WLS-estimated factor (\(f\_se / f\)). sigma Mack sigma (residual standard deviation WLS fit). Used Mack variance estimation. n_cohorts Total number observations link. n_valid Number finite ata values. n_inf Number infinite ata values. n_nan Number NaN ata values. valid_ratio Proportion finite ata values (\(n\_valid / n\_cohorts\)).

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: wt f weighted averages observed ata factors, differ weights assigned observations included: wt Volume-weighted mean: \(wt = \sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\). Computed rows loss_from loss_to finite, including rows either value zero. Independent alpha. f WLS-estimated factor. rows loss_from > 0 used, since loss_from = 0 causes numerical issues WLS weights (\(w = value\_from^{\alpha}\)). alpha = 2, f wt numerically equivalent (assuming zero loss_from rows). alpha \ne 2, diverge. Therefore wt f can differ two reasons: Zero exclusion: rows loss_from = 0 included wt excluded f. typically affects early development periods cohorts yet accumulated claims. Alpha effect: alpha \ne 2, WLS weights differ volume weights used wt, leading different estimates. Comparing wt f can help diagnose whether choice alpha materially affects estimated factor.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: input "Link" object contains weight column (added as_link() weight supplied), column automatically used WLS weight place loss_from. useful loss = "ratio", loss_from carries premium information external premium variable premium used instead.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: coefficient variation defined : $$cv = \frac{SD(f_k)}{\bar{f}_k}$$ \(f_k\) individual observed ata values link \(k\) \(\bar{f}_k\) arithmetic mean. cv reflects relative spread observed factors across cohorts, regardless premium scale. used detect_maturity() one criteria determining maturity point.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: relative standard error defined : $$rse = \frac{SE(\hat{f}_k)}{\hat{f}_k}$$ \(SE(\hat{f}_k)\) standard error WLS-estimated factor. Unlike cv, treats cohorts equally, rse gives weight cohorts larger premiums (via WLS weights). small rse indicates WLS estimate precise, tends occur : (1) many cohorts, (2) premiums large, (3) observed ata values consistent across cohorts.

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
text: Internal helper computes group-wise summary statistics incremental loss intensity \(g\) dual-variable Link object (built exposure set). Dispatched via summary.Link() model = "ed". Two purposes: Diagnostics: provides descriptive statistics (mean, median, wt, cv) help user assess stability consistency observed \(g\) values across cohorts. Estimation: fits -intercept weighted least squares model per development link produce WLS-estimated intensity (g), standard error (g_se), relative standard error (rse), residual sigma (sigma). used downstream fit_ed().

Independent alpha. g WLS-estimated intensity lm(loss_delta ~ premium_from + 0). rows premium_from > 0 used. alpha = 2, g wt numerically equivalent.

title: Unified theme dispatcher (internal) — .switch_theme
text: Produces ggplot2::theme() configured one three usage contexts: "view": RStudio screen exploration; sizes inherit ggplot2 defaults. "save": embedding spreadsheet tools Excel; fixed axis / title sizes, plot background untouched. "shiny": embedding Shiny apps; "save" plus configurable transparent plot background. Setting *.size argument 0 replaces element ggplot2::element_blank().

title: Bornhuetter-Ferguson projection — fit_bf
text: Fit Bornhuetter-Ferguson (1972) projection "Triangle" object. BF estimator blends observed cumulative loss cohort priori expected loss ratio (ELR) applied cohort's ultimate premium, weighted expected unemerged fraction \(1 - q_i\): $$\hat L_{ult, }^{BF} = L_{obs, } +   (1 - q_i) \cdot \mathrm{ELR}_i \cdot E_i^{ult}$$ \(L_{obs, }\): cohort \(\)'s observed cumulative loss latest observed development period. \(q_i = L_{obs, } / \hat L_{ult, }^{CL}\): expected emerged fraction, equivalent inverse cumulative loss development factor (LDF) cohort \(\). \(\mathrm{ELR}_i\): user-supplied priori expected loss ratio cohort \(\) (prior argument). \(E_i^{ult}\): cohort \(\)'s ultimate premium, projected via chain ladder premium column. peer worker alongside fit_cl() / fit_ed() / fit_loss(). Standalone BF recipe – composition fit_ratio() part worker. Point projection always computed; bootstrap SE / CI opt-via bootstrap = TRUE (Phase 3b). Closed-form Mack (2008) MSEP yet implemented.

title: Bornhuetter-Ferguson projection — fit_bf
code: fit_bf(   x,   loss = "loss",   exposure = "premium",   prior,   bootstrap = NULL,   B = 999L,   seed = NULL,   type = c("parametric", "nonparametric", "analytical"),   residual = c("cell", "link"),   process = c("gamma", "od_pois", "normal"),   alpha = 1,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none"),   recent = NULL,   regime = NULL,   maturity = NULL,   credibility = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Bornhuetter-Ferguson projection — fit_bf
text: x Triangle object. loss single cumulative loss variable project. Default "loss". exposure single cumulative premium variable used denominator prior ELR. Default "premium". prior priori expected loss ratio. Accepts: single numeric Applied uniformly every cohort. per-cohort data.frame (cohort + elr) Per-cohort ELR. Must cover every cohort present x (extras silently dropped, missing cohorts raise error). per-group data.frame (grouping columns + elr) One ELR per group, broadcast every cohort group. Useful single priori ELR set per line business. Must cover every group present x. data.frame prior may also carry optional elr_se column – standard error priori ELR (distribution prior). supplied, bootstrap path draws per-replicate ELR Normal(elr, elr_se) instead treating prior fixed point. Omit (leave NA) deterministic prior. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration. Five forms accepted: NULL / FALSE (default) Point estimate – bootstrap SE/CI. TRUE / "auto" Internal bootstrap() calls (one loss, one premium) sharing seed replicate indices align across two simulations. Named list list(loss = BootstrapTriangle, premium = BootstrapTriangle) Pre-built objects bootstrap(). Must matching meta$B / meta$seed per-replicate composition well-defined; meta$target must "loss" "premium" respectively. Function function(tri) -> list(loss = ..., premium = ...) Lazy spec invoked input Triangle (leakage-safe backtest()). Latest observed cumulative loss perturbed BF recipe – treated cohort anchor, mirroring point-estimate formula. B Integer number bootstrap replicates. Used bootstrap resolves "auto". Default 999. seed Optional integer seed reproducible bootstrap. Default NULL. type One "parametric" (default), "nonparametric", "analytical". "parametric" / "nonparametric" select bootstrap residual paradigm; "analytical" skips simulation uses closed-form Mack (2008) BF MSEP decomposition cohort-level SE / CI. bootstrap requested analytical path used regardless type. residual Residual scope type = "nonparametric". One "cell" (default) "link". See bootstrap(). process One "gamma" (default), "od_pois", "normal". See bootstrap(). alpha Numeric scalar passed inner fit_cl() fit_premium() calls. Default 1. sigma_method Sigma extrapolation method forwarded fit_cl() / fit_premium(). Default "locf". recent Optional positive integer; calendar-diagonal filter forwarded inner fits. Default NULL. regime Optional regime specification forwarded inner loss premium fits. See fit_cl() four-type dispatch. maturity Optional maturity specification forwarded inner loss fit. See fit_cl() four-type dispatch. credibility Optional credibility specification. NULL (default) gives classical BF blend weight equal emergence fraction q. list list(method = "bs", K = NULL) switches Buehlmann-Straub credibility blend ult = Z * CL + (1 - Z) * prior, Z = K / (K + s^2), s^2 variance cohort's CL loss-ratio estimate, K variance hypothetical means (genuine -cohort spread). K estimated per group NULL, supplied non-negative numeric scalar. credibility weight protects rare-event cohorts: green cohort CL estimate built almost data large s^2, Z shrinks toward 0 cohort pulled prior even q high. credibility blend always uses analytical SE path (SE approximate – credibility factor treated fixed plug-). conf_level Confidence level bootstrap quantile CI loss_ult. Default 0.95. ... Reserved future extension (currently unused).

title: Cape Cod projection (Stanard 1985) — fit_cc
text: Fit Cape Cod projection "Triangle" object. Cape Cod prior-free Bornhuetter-Ferguson variant introduced Stanard (1985): priori expected loss ratio estimated data portfolio-pooled quantity, plugged BF formula. $$\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC} =   \frac{\sum_i L_{obs, }}{\sum_i E_i^{ult} \cdot q_i}$$ \(L_{obs, }\): cohort \(\)'s observed cumulative loss latest observed development period. \(q_i = L_{obs, } / \hat L_{ult, }^{CL}\): expected emerged fraction (inverse cumulative LDF). \(E_i^{ult}\): cohort \(\)'s ultimate premium (projected via chain ladder premium). Given \(\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC}\), per-cohort ultimate obtained BF formula single pooled ELR: $$\hat L_{ult, }^{CC} = L_{obs, } +   (1 - q_i) \cdot \widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC} \cdot E_i^{ult}$$ multiple groups present, \(\widehat{\mathrm{ELR}}^{CC}\) computed within group (pooled across groups) group retains portfolio-level ELR estimate. peer worker alongside fit_bf() / fit_cl() / fit_ed(). Standalone Cape Cod recipe – composition fit_ratio() part worker. Point projection always computed; bootstrap SE / CI opt-via bootstrap = TRUE (Phase 3b). bootstrap path also produces per-replicate pooled ELR draws (elr_cc_se, elr_cc_cv, elr_cc_ci_lo, elr_cc_ci_hi) since Cape Cod ELR data-driven thus uncertain.

what: Fit a chain ladder projection on the premium triangle — fit_premium
title: Fit a chain ladder projection on the premium triangle — fit_premium
text: Project cumulative premium across cohort x development grid chain ladder estimator. Two variance recursions supported: "ed" (default) Additive recursion. Empirically robust long-projection premium triangles – multiplicative scaling classical CL recursion can blow cohort-wise heterogeneity (regime changes premium, channel changes, amendments). See dev/premium_projection.qmd. "cl" Mack (1993) multiplicative recursion. Point projection identical ED; SE accumulation differs. methods share point estimate – self-weighted ED premium mathematically equivalent chain ladder column (f_k = 1 + g_k). operational difference cumulative variance propagated forward.

what: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
text: Unified interface loss ratio projection "Triangle" object. Five projection methods available: "ed" (default) Exposure-driven development periods. future increments \(g_k \cdot C^P_k\). Unconditional safe baseline – maturity dependency, robust early-dev ATA volatility. "cl" Chain ladder development periods. Equivalent classical Mack (1993) recursion. "sa" Stage-adaptive composition: ED maturity, CL maturity. Requires maturity detection (2-pass), uses age-- age factors stabilised. maturity: age--age factors volatile, exposure-driven projection \(\Delta C^L = g_k \cdot C^P_k\) anchors estimate premium volume. maturity: age--age factors stable, chain ladder projection \(C^L_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C^L_k\) preserves cohort's observed level. "bf" Bornhuetter-Ferguson: blends chain-ladder ultimate external prior loss ratio emergence fraction. Requires prior argument, passed .... "cc" Cape Cod: like "bf", prior loss ratio estimated pooling data rather supplied externally. cases, premium projected forward ultimate; point projection identical premium_method = "ed" "cl" (differ variance recursion). function composition layer fit_loss() fit_premium(): delegates loss projection fit_loss(), retrieves embedded PremiumFit, composes loss-ratio point + variance via delta method (se_method = "fixed" "delta"). See ARCHITECTURE.md layered design.

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
code: fit_ratio(   x,   method = c("ed", "cl", "sa", "bf", "cc"),   loss_alpha = 1,   loss_regime = NULL,   premium_method = c("ed", "cl"),   premium_alpha = 1,   premium_regime = NULL,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none"),   recent = NULL,   maturity = "auto",   tail = FALSE,   se_method = c("fixed", "delta"),   rho = 0.95,   conf_level = 0.95,   bootstrap = NULL,   B = 999L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_ratio
text: x object class "Triangle". standardized "loss" "premium" columns used (as_triangle() produces ). method One "ed" (default), "cl", "sa", "bf", "cc" – loss-side projection method, forwarded fit_loss(). "bf" "cc" require prior arguments (prior, etc.) supplied .... loss_alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure loss estimation. Default 1. loss_regime Optional regime specification loss-side filter. Accepts four input types: NULL (default) regime filter. Regime object Use -. Typically built via detect_regime() regime_at(). "auto" Detect regime internally via detect_regime(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Regime object (NULL). Behavior depends method: "sa" Hybrid filter. Pre-change cohorts dropped development periods maturity point (ED phase); post-maturity (CL) cells use recent-diagonal window across cohorts. preserves CL stability protecting ED intensities regime change. "ed", "cl" Simple cohort cut: cohorts strictly change date excluded estimation. premium_method One "ed" (default) "cl". Forwarded fit_premium() constructing premium projection. premium_alpha Numeric scalar premium chain ladder. Default 1. premium_regime Premium-side regime specification. four input types loss_regime (NULL / Regime / "auto" / function). Default NULL – premium fit full triangle independently loss_regime (lazy default). Set explicitly regime shift affects premium accrual . sigma_method Method used extrapolate sigma links estimated. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none". "mack" applies Mack (1993, Appendix B) tail estimator last unestimated link , falling back LOCF earlier ones warning. "none" performs extrapolation; sigma stays NA downstream variance terms drop links via finite-value guards. Passed .extrapolate_sigma_ata(). recent Optional positive integer estimation window. Default NULL. maturity Optional maturity specification. Accepts four input types: NULL maturity filter. Disables SA-mode switch detection. Maturity object Use -. Typically built via detect_maturity() maturity_at(). "auto" (default) Detect maturity internally via detect_maturity(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Maturity object (e.g. maturity_spec()) deferred custom-config detection. method = "sa", also determines switch point ED CL phases. tail Tail factor loss-side projection, forwarded fit_loss(). FALSE (default) applies none; TRUE estimates log-linear tail factor; numeric value used directly. se_method Method computing ratio_se = SE(L/P). One : "fixed" (default) Premium treated fixed (non-random). \(\mathrm{SE}(L/P) = \mathrm{SE}(L) / P\). Strictly, delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0, .e., degenerate case assumption premium known. "delta" Full delta method including premium uncertainty loss-premium correlation rho: $$\mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{P^2}         + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(P)}{P^4}         - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P)}{P^3}$$ rho Numeric scalar (-1, 1); assumed correlation ultimate loss ultimate premium. used se_method = "delta". Default 0.95, matching strong positive correlation typically observed cumulative loss cumulative premium long-tail health portfolios (analogous paid/incurred correlation used Munich chain ladder). conf_level Confidence level used ratio_ci_lo/ratio_ci_hi cohort summary. Default 0.95. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration. Five forms accepted: NULL (default) Auto-resolved method: bootstrap "sa"/"ed", analytical "cl"/"bf"/"cc". TRUE / FALSE Back-compat legacy logical arg. TRUE triggers "auto"; FALSE disables. "auto" Internal bootstrap() call loss triangle defaults (type = "analytical", process = "normal", target = "loss"). BootstrapTriangle Pre-built object bootstrap(). Must meta$target == "loss". Function function(tri) -> BootstrapTriangle Lazy spec invoked input Triangle (leakage-safe backtest()). Premium held observed values bootstrap (loss-convention). ratio_se recomputed bootstrap-derived loss_total_se via .compute_ratio_se(), combined premium-side SE per se_method ("fixed" ignores premium SE; "delta" uses premium_total_se inner fit_premium() plus rho correlation). B Integer number bootstrap replications. Used bootstrap resolves "auto". Default 999. seed Optional integer seed reproducible bootstrap. Default NULL. ... Additional arguments forwarded fit_loss() worker – notably prior (related arguments) method = "bf" "cc".

what: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
title: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
text: Project cumulative loss across cohort x development grid using stage-adaptive (SA) method: ED maturity point, CL . SA composes projection paradigms anchored per-group maturity switch – 2-pass fit (maturity detection via fit_ata(), SA projection ). SA worker – standalone, internal method dispatch. role-specific entry point fit_loss() dispatches method = "sa" function; users can also call fit_sa() directly.

title: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
code: fit_sa(   x,   loss = "loss",   exposure = "premium",   alpha = 1,   exposure_fit = NULL,   exposure_method = c("ed", "cl"),   exposure_alpha = 1,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none"),   recent = NULL,   regime = NULL,   maturity = "auto",   tail = FALSE,   conf_level = 0.95,   bootstrap = NULL,   B = 999L,   seed = NULL,   type = c("parametric", "nonparametric", "analytical") )

title: Fit stage-adaptive (SA) loss projection on a Triangle — fit_sa
text: x "Triangle" object. standardized "loss" "premium" columns used (as_triangle() produces ). loss Cumulative loss column name. Default "loss". exposure Cumulative premium column name. Default "premium". alpha Variance-structure exponent loss fit. Default 1. exposure_fit Optional pre-built PremiumFit supplying premium projection. NULL, fit_sa() builds premium projection internally – worker-layer fit_cl() premium column – using exposure_method, exposure_alpha, resolved regime. exposure_method One "ed" (default) "cl". Used exposure_fit = NULL. exposure_alpha Variance-structure exponent premium fit. Default 1. sigma_method Method used extrapolate sigma links estimated. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear", "mack", "none". "mack" applies Mack (1993, Appendix B) tail estimator last unestimated link , falling back LOCF earlier ones warning. "none" performs extrapolation; sigma stays NA downstream variance terms drop links via finite-value guards. Passed .extrapolate_sigma_ata(). recent Optional positive integer; calendar-diagonal filter. regime Optional regime specification (loss-side). Accepts standard 4-type dispatch (NULL / Regime / "auto" / function). SA mode resolved regime drives hybrid 2-pass filter (cohort cut ED phase, calendar-diagonal wedge CL phase). maturity Maturity specification. Default "auto". Accepts standard 4-type dispatch (NULL / Maturity / "auto" / function). SA requires maturity – NULL disables SA entirely (use ED CL directly case). tail Logical numeric; tail factor CL phase. Forwarded internal premium fit relevant. conf_level Confidence level analytical CI loss projection. Default 0.95. bootstrap Bootstrap configuration (NULL / TRUE / FALSE / "auto" / BootstrapTriangle / lazy function). Default NULL resolves "auto" (residual bootstrap) SA. B Integer number bootstrap replicates. Default 999. seed Optional integer seed. type Bootstrap process type. Default "parametric". (used bootstrap = "auto".)

what: lossratio: Loss Ratio Analytics for Long-Term Health Insurance — lossratio-package
title: lossratio: Loss Ratio Analytics for Long-Term Health Insurance — lossratio-package
text: loss ratio analytics toolkit long-term health insurance, covering cohort development analysis, stage-adaptive loss-ratio projection, regime detection, backtest validation long-format experience data. Implements stage-adaptive loss-ratio projection uses exposure-driven model maturity point chain ladder , supporting maturity point detection cohort regime detection handling structural breaks. Provides cohort development period, calendar period, total aggregation frameworks diagnostics, applies cumulative loss premium setting sufficient sample per cell.

what: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: Captures detect_maturity() arguments without running detection. Returns closure consumer (fit_* backtest()) invokes internal triangle. point conditional / deferred detection – value $k^*$ depends cells caller decides expose: fit_ratio() / fit_loss(), spec invoked full triangle user passed . backtest(), spec invoked masked triangle holdout fold, never full triangle. Held-diagonals removed detect_maturity() sees data, detected $k^*$ depends cells masked fit can also see. leakage-safe contract maturity_spec(). Contrast maturity_at(), produces eager "Maturity" object whose value fixed construction time (independent fold's masked data). Use maturity_spec() want $k^*$ re-detected per fold backtest honestly answers "given data available fold, picked?" Use maturity_at() want fixed value tested across folds.

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: ... kwargs passed verbatim detect_maturity() spec invoked (e.g. loss, groups, min_run, max_cv, max_rse, min_valid_ratio, min_n_valid).

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: function one argument ("Triangle") returning "Maturity" object. caller decides triangle pass (full vs. masked); inside backtest() always masked training triangle.

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Capture detection arguments, defer execution until fit time. spec <- maturity_spec(min_run = 2, max_cv = 0.04)  # In fit_ratio(): closure is invoked on the user's `tri`. fit <- fit_ratio(tri, maturity = maturity_spec(min_run = 2))  # In backtest(): closure is invoked on the *masked* triangle of # each holdout fold, so detected k* never peeks at held-out cells. bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L,                maturity = maturity_spec(min_run = 2, max_cv = 0.04)) } # }

text: x object class Triangle. view Plot view. One : "value" (default) Per-cell metric heatmap controlled metric, label_style, amount_divisor, nrow, ncol. "usage" Cell-status heatmap (used / holdout / unused / future). Accepts recent, regime, holdout, maturity via .... See vignette("diagnostics") details. metric single metric plot. Must one : "ratio", "incr_ratio", "loss", "incr_loss", "premium", "incr_premium", "margin", "incr_margin", "loss_share", "incr_loss_share", "premium_share", "incr_premium_share". label_style Label display style. One : "value" Show selected metric. "detail" ratio / incr_ratio, show ratio percent , next line, associated loss / premium amounts. amount proportion metrics, falls back "value". label_size Numeric size -cell text label. Defaults 3 label_style = "value" 2.5 label_style = "detail" (two-line labels need smaller size fit). label appearance fields (family, color, hjust, ...) fall back standard label defaults. amount_divisor Numeric scaling factor applied amount variables (e.g., loss, incr_loss, premium, incr_premium, margin, incr_margin) plotting. Default "auto" picks largest divisor {1, 1e3, 1e6, 1e7, 1e8, 1e9} median displayed value still least 1, minimising label digit count. nrow, ncol Number rows columns passed ggplot2::facet_wrap(). theme string passed .switch_theme() ("view", "save", "shiny"). ... what: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: User-facing helper hand-specifying regime change (set per-group changes) without running detect_regime(). returned "Regime" object plugs function consumes Regime – fit_ratio(), fit_loss(), fit_premium(), backtest(), regime-change resolver – carrying $changes schema detect_regime() output. Argument syntax mirrors data.frame() / data.table(): named vectors equal length, one must change. named arguments treated group columns.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: ... Named vectors equal length. Must include change (coercible Date; start--regime date post-change regime). named arguments interpreted group column values (e.g. coverage, channel). group columns result pooled (single-row) Regime. treatment downstream fits apply Regime $changes contains multiple change points. "segment_bridged" (default) pools bridged development band single factor estimate. "segment_bridged_borrowed" estimates per-segment factors borrows late-dev factors segment reach. mask bridged band every cohort projects full development. See detect_regime() full semantics.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: object class "Regime" minimal schema needed downstream consumers: method "manual". loss NA_character_ (detection metric). changes data.table columns [<group cols>..., change, regime_id, pre_value, post_value, magnitude]. regime_id 2L (post-change regime) row; stats columns NA_real_. groups Character vector group column names (possibly empty). multi_group TRUE group columns one unique group row. Detection-specific slots (labels, trajectory, pca, dropped, n_regimes, window, window_mode, pca) left empty / NA object can still printed consumed clearly distinguishable detected Regime.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pooled change (no group columns) regime_at(change = "2024-07-01")  # Single-group change regime_at(coverage = "surgery", change = "2024-04-01")  # Multiple groups, one column regime_at(coverage = c("surgery", "cancer"),           change   = c("2024-04-01", "2023-09-01"))  # Multi-dimensional group keys regime_at(coverage = c("surgery", "surgery"),           channel  = c("online", "agent"),           change   = c("2024-04-01", "2024-05-01")) } # }

what: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: Captures detect_regime() arguments without running detection. Returns closure consumer (fit_* backtest()) invokes internal triangle. point conditional / deferred detection – change points depend cells caller decides expose: fit_ratio() / fit_loss() / fit_premium(), spec invoked full triangle user passed . backtest(), spec invoked masked triangle holdout fold, never full triangle. Held-diagonals removed detect_regime() sees data, detected change points depend cells masked fit can also see. leakage-safe contract regime_spec(). Contrast regime_at(), produces eager "Regime" object whose change points fixed construction time (independent fold's masked data). Use regime_spec() want change points re-detected per fold backtest honestly answers "given data available fold, regime structure picked?" Use regime_at() want fixed regime tested across folds.

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: ... kwargs passed verbatim detect_regime() spec invoked (e.g. loss, , min_run, method).

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: function one argument ("Triangle") returning "Regime" object. caller decides triangle pass (full vs. masked); inside backtest() always masked training triangle.

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Capture detection arguments, defer execution until fit time. spec <- regime_spec(loss = "loss_ata")  # In fit_ratio(): closure is invoked on the user's `tri`. fit <- fit_ratio(tri, loss_regime = regime_spec(loss = "loss_ata"))  # In backtest(): closure is invoked on the *masked* triangle of # each holdout fold, so detected change points never peek at # held-out cells. bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L,                loss_regime = regime_spec(loss = "loss_ata")) } # }

what: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
title: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
text: render() prints data frame, data table, Triangle object compact, fixed-width console table. rendered table four parts: shape: (rows, cols) header; box-drawn grid carrying column-name row column-type row (<int>, <dbl>, <date>, <chr>, ...); head / tail sample rows – object n rows middle collapsed single ellipsis row; tibble-style "N variables" footer listing columns dropped table wide console. grid, shape: header, per-column type row follow dataframe console layout package's Python sibling, lossratio-py; truncated-columns footer follows tibble print convention. adopted lossratio previews stay visually consistent wider R Python tabular ecosystems. Columns selected ends inward fill width; dropped middle columns summarised footer. print.Triangle() delegates render(), Triangle objects print style default.

title: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
code: render(x, ...)  # Default S3 method render(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'data.table' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'tbl_df' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Triangle' render(   x,   n = 10,   width = getOption("width", 100),   max_col_width = 14,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Triangle' print(x, ...)

title: Render a tabular object as a compact console table — render
text: x object render. Methods provided data frames, data tables, tibbles, Triangle objects; default method falls back print(). ... Additional arguments passed methods. n Integer; maximum number data rows show head / tail sampling applies. Default 10. width Integer; target console width characters. Default getOption("width", 100). max_col_width Integer; maximum width single column – longer values truncated ellipsis. Default 14. verbose Logical; whether print "variables" footer columns dropped fit width. Default TRUE.

what: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: Check (groups, cohort) cohort consecutive dev sequence within observed range. Non-consecutive cohorts produce non-consecutive age--age links downstream (e.g., 14 -> 17 instead 14 -> 15), breaks summary.Link() key uniqueness causes cartesian joins fit_ratio(). function inspects raw data without modifying . Use as_triangle() decide whether fix data source, drop offending cohorts, pass fill_gaps = TRUE as_triangle(). Two checks performed: Cohort dev-sequence gaps – (group, cohort), report missing dev values within observed range. Row-level calendar consistency – calendar supplied, report rows calendar < cohort. rows logically impossible (claims precede policy issue) downstream show negative dev_m, polluting cohort dev sequences.

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
code: validate_triangle(   df,   groups = NULL,   cohort,   calendar = NULL,   dev = NULL,   grain = "auto" )

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: df data.frame. groups Grouping variable(s). cohort single cohort variable (raw column name). calendar Optional calendar period variable row-level consistency check. supplied, rows calendar < cohort flagged invalid. Default NULL (skip check). dev single development-period variable (raw column name). Optional calendar supplied – dev derived (cohort, calendar) resolved grain (dispatch as_triangle()). grain Grain string ("M" / "Q" / "H" / "Y") "auto" (default) – used dev derived (cohort, calendar). Ignored dev supplied.

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: data.table class "TriangleValidation" one row per cohort containing gaps. Columns: groups, cohort Cohort identifier. n_dev Number distinct observed dev values. n_expected max(dev) - min(dev) + 1 cohort. missing List column missing dev values. Returns zero-row data.table gaps found. Row-level violations (calendar supplied check finds ) attached "invalid_rows" attribute – data.table columns [groups, cohort, calendar, dev (present), reason]. Use attr(, "invalid_rows") rely print.TriangleValidation displays sections.

title: lossratio (development version)
text: BREAKING: Regime treatment redesign – two full-development treatments. treatment enum now c("segment_bridged", "segment_bridged_borrowed") (default "segment_bridged"). previous values "latest_only", "segment_wise", "segment_wise_bridged" removed: either failed project newest cohorts full development ("latest_only" shortens horizon surviving subset; "segment_wise" leaves newest segment’s late-dev cells unreachable) half-step toward redesign. new treatments mask triangle bridged development band – per-segment mini-triangle wall (dev >= max_cal - seg_last + 1) widened calendar-diagonal bridge next segment’s first-cohort midpoint dev – closes factor gaps segment boundaries continuous run age--age factors covers every development period every cohort projects full development length. "segment_bridged" (default) pools whole band single factor set (development pattern shared across regimes; band’s lower boundary regime-aware). masked band drops segment_id tag downstream estimation pooled. "segment_bridged_borrowed" estimates factors per segment (early-dev factors stay regime-specific) borrows late-dev factors segment reach donor segment can (recent segment developed far). New helper .borrow_segment_factors() performs projection-time augmentation fit_cl() / fit_ed(). band mask applied Triangle (cohort x dev grid) Link built, Link omits dev-1-cohorts corrupt segment’s last-cohort rank. cohort-cut mechanism backed "latest_only" survives internally stage-adaptive (fit_sa()) hybrid filter, user-facing treatment. Helper .compute_segment_mini_tri_bounds() still computes per-cell band dev_min shared .apply_regime_filter() .compute_triangle_usage(). BREAKING: identifier rename exposure -> premium. denominator slot reverts premium, natural domain word loss-ratio analytics (loss ratio = loss / premium). earlier framework-generic choice exposure (still unreleased) superseded: “cumulative exposure” / “incremental exposure” read non-standard insurance reserving context, whereas “cumulative premium” / “incremental premium” immediately clear. derived ratio column / fit family unchanged. Prose noun phrases (“loss ratio”, “premium”, “risk premium”, “premium measure”) unchanged, exposure-driven (ED) method keeps name. Migration (find--replace call sites): as_triangle(..., exposure = "incr_exposure") -> as_triangle(..., premium = "incr_premium") fit_exposure(...) -> fit_premium(...) fit_loss(..., exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ..., exposure_fit = ...) -> fit_loss(..., premium_method = ..., premium_alpha = ..., premium_fit = ...) fit_ratio(..., exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ..., exposure_regime = ...) -> fit_ratio(..., premium_method = ..., premium_alpha = ..., premium_regime = ...) backtest(..., target = "exposure", exposure_method = ...) -> backtest(..., target = "premium", premium_method = ...) bootstrap(tri, target = "exposure") -> bootstrap(tri, target = "premium") RatioFit$exposure_alpha, $exposure_regime -> $premium_alpha, $premium_regime LossFit$exposure_fit -> $premium_fit S3 class "ExposureFit" -> "PremiumFit" (incl. print.ExposureFit() / summary.ExposureFit() -> print.PremiumFit() / summary.PremiumFit()) attr(ExposureFit_obj, "exposure_method") -> attr(PremiumFit_obj, "premium_method") Triangle / Calendar / Total columns: exposure, incr_exposure, exposure_share, incr_exposure_share -> premium, incr_premium, premium_share, incr_premium_share Fit output columns: exposure_proj, exposure_obs, exposure_proc_se, exposure_param_se, exposure_total_se, exposure_total_cv, exposure_ci_lo, exposure_ci_hi, incr_exposure_proj -> premium_* R source file: R/exposure.R -> R/premium.R Raw dataset (data/experience.rda): column incr_exposure -> incr_premium (regenerated) maturity_from column reported method = "sa" fits (summary() underlying $full table) now carries maturity link’s -index (ata_to, first CL-phase dev) instead -index, matching package convention maturity point link target dev. Projections, standard errors, confidence intervals unchanged – reported column value shifts one. API consistency pass. Several entry points aligned concept behaves way regardless entry point: premium_method now defaults "ed" ("cl") fit_sa() / fit_loss() / fit_ratio() / backtest(), matching fit_premium(). changes default premium-side variance recursion; point projections unaffected. Pass premium_method = "cl" keep old behaviour. bootstrap()’s type now defaults "parametric" ("analytical"), matching fit_sa() / fit_bf() / fit_cc() workers. Pass type = "analytical" closed-form path. fit_ratio() backtest() accept "bf" "cc" loss-side methods (forwarded fit_loss()); supply prior arguments .... fit_ratio() gained tail argument, forwarded fit_loss(). Buehlmann-Straub credibility blend fit_bf() / fit_cc(). new credibility argument switches BF / CC blend weight emergence fraction q Buehlmann-Straub credibility factor Z = K / (K + s^2), s^2 variance cohort’s CL loss-ratio estimate K -cohort variance true loss ratios (estimated per group, supplied). credibility = NULL (default) keeps classical blend. credibility weight protects rare-event green cohorts: CL estimate built almost data large s^2, Z shrinks toward 0 cohort pulled prior even q high. fit carries $credibility slot per-cohort Z / K. Analytical prediction error fit_bf() / fit_cc(). type = "analytical" now implemented (previously stub error). computes closed-form mean squared error prediction via Mack (2008) Bornhuetter-Ferguson MSEP decomposition – process error plus development-pattern prior estimation error – without simulation. $summary carries loss_total_se / loss_total_cv / loss_ci_lo / loss_ci_hi; fit_cc() additionally reports elr_cc_se / elr_cc_cv / elr_cc_ci_lo / elr_cc_ci_hi data-estimated pooled ELR. analytical path also used whenever bootstrap requested, every fit now reports SE. Distribution prior fit_bf(). data.frame prior may carry optional elr_se column – standard error priori ELR. bootstrap path draws per-replicate ELR Normal(elr, elr_se), analytical path feeds Var(ELR) term. deterministic prior (elr_se) unchanged. Per-group prior fit_bf(). prior data.frame may carry grouping columns plus elr without cohort column; group’s ELR broadcast every cohort group. Worker layer fix + bootstrap arg fit_cl / fit_ed. fit_bf() / fit_cc() / fit_sa() now build internal premium fit calling fit_cl(loss = "premium", ...) directly instead routing fit_premium() — downward-Tier 3 -> Tier 4 dependency. fit_cl() fit_ed() gain bootstrap, B, seed, conf_level arguments symmetry SA / BF / CC workers; bootstrap = NULL (default) preserves analytical Mack SE. fit-result classes standardised c("XFit", "list") (prepended forms dispatchers). fit_bf() + fit_cc() promoted peer workers bootstrap composition. fit_bf takes external prior ELR (Bornhuetter-Ferguson 1972); fit_cc derives ELR data via payout weighting (Stanard 1985, Cape Cod). expose bootstrap = "auto" cell / link / parametric simulation analytical fallback. Promotion makes available fit_loss(method = "bf" | "cc"). fit_sa worker + fit_loss true dispatcher. Phase 4 split stage-adaptive composition (R/sa.R, class "SAFit") R/loss.R, fit_loss() now thin-dispatches method worker functions (fit_ed / fit_cl / fit_sa / fit_bf / fit_cc) augments output LossFit-uniform $full schema via .lossfit_augment(). fit_premium() follows pattern .premiumfit_augment() + .premiumfit_bootstrap() (Phase 4c). BREAKING: bootstrap type = "parametric" -> "analytical" rename. Mack closed-form SE option previously mislabelled "parametric". textbook-parametric kernels (cell-distribution sampling + refit, England-Verrall 1999) now use type = "parametric", analytical Mack closed-form lives type = "analytical". bootstrap.Triangle() default "analytical"; worker-side fit_sa / fit_bf / fit_cc type = defaults "parametric" (cell-distribution simulation). SA nonparametric bootstrap proper kernel. bootstrap(method = "sa") previously silently dispatched CL cell kernel. Phase 1 introduced dedicated bootstrap_kernel_sa_cell (link variants) respect stage transition maturity k^*, ED-stage cells using additive g_k refit CL-stage cells using multiplicative f_k refit. ED bootstrap (Phase 1, fixed premium). bootstrap() now supports method = "ed" residual = "cell": per-replicate g*_k refit additive forward projection (Delta loss = g_k * P_{} + noise) instead multiplicative chain ladder. Premium stays fixed across replicates (projected via CL premium column). New native helpers bootstrap_refit_gstar / bootstrap_fwd_proj_ed_and_clip / bootstrap_fwd_sim_ed_cell parallel CL kernel triple; C entry point C_bootstrap_kernel_ed_cell (17 args). Phase 2 / 3 (joint loss + premium bootstrap) deferred. method = "ed" requires residual = "cell"; ED + link residuals implemented. bootstrap() method-enum reorder — c("sa", "cl", "ed") -> c("ed", "cl", "sa"). Matches fit_loss() / fit_ratio() default flip. Default now "ed". Users relying "sa" bootstrap method must pass explicitly. BREAKING: method default flip — "sa" -> "ed". fit_loss(), fit_ratio(), backtest() (via loss_method) now default method = "ed" (exposure-driven) instead "sa" (stage-adaptive). Method-enum order c("ed", "cl", "sa") — simple -> classical -> composition. Rationale: ED unconditional safe baseline (additive, maturity-detection dependency, robust early-dev age--age volatility); CL classical Mack 1993 alternative; SA composition ED + CL requiring 2-pass maturity detection. Users relying stage-adaptive behaviour must now pass method = "sa" explicitly. Migration: fit_loss(tri) -> still works (now defaults ED) fit_loss(tri, method = "sa") -> explicit (change) Want previous SA default behaviour back? -> add method = "sa" Variance helper rename — .mack_g_var -> .ed_g_var. Internal factor-level variance helper renamed paradigm clarity. .mack_* reserved CL/Mack 1993 paradigm (f-factor variance); ED intensity variance follows Buehlmann-Straub 1970 lineage now lives .ed_g_var(). two natural analytical variance helpers package now: .mack_f_var() (CL paradigm, f) .ed_g_var() (ED paradigm, g). Cross-paradigm pairs provided separate functions — algebraically derivable via g_k = f_k - 1. helpers now carry @references blocks citing Mack (1993) Buehlmann-Straub (1970) respectively. BREAKING: worker-arg rename target -> loss. Worker-layer functions (fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_ata, fit_intensity, detect_maturity, detect_regime) as_link() now take loss argument place target. Worker-output columns rename accordingly (target_obs -> loss_obs, target_proj -> loss_proj, target_*_se -> loss_*_se, target_from / target_to / target_delta -> loss_from / loss_to / loss_delta). Fit-object attribute key attr(., "target") becomes attr(., "loss") Link, ATAFit, EDFit, CLFit, IntensityFit, Maturity, Regime, Convergence objects. target arg backtest() (dispatcher enum selecting "ratio" / "loss" / "premium") unchanged — different semantic (metric backtest) stays -. Bootstrap’s target = c("loss", "premium") enum also unchanged. Migration: fit_cl(tri, target = "loss") -> fit_cl(tri, loss = "loss") fit_ed(tri, target = "loss", premium = ...) -> fit_ed(tri, loss = "loss", premium = ...) as_link(tri, target = "loss") -> as_link(tri, loss = "loss") detect_maturity(tri, target = "loss") -> detect_maturity(tri, loss = "loss") detect_regime(tri, target = "ratio") -> detect_regime(tri, loss = "ratio") Reading cl_fit$full$target_proj -> cl_fit$full$loss_proj Reading attr(link, "target") -> attr(link, "loss") BREAKING: identifier rename prem -> exposure, lr -> ratio. Framework-generic naming denominator slot (exposure) derived ratio column / fit family (ratio). previous -progress sweep premium -> prem (still unreleased) superseded — final target exposure, framework-generic word covers loss reserving (risk premium = exposure), frequency (insureds = exposure), severity (claim count = Bühlmann natural weight = exposure) uniformly. Prose noun phrases (“loss ratio”, “premium”, “risk premium”, “exposure measure”) unchanged. Migration (find--replace call sites): as_triangle(..., premium = "incr_prem") / as_triangle(..., prem = "incr_prem") -> as_triangle(..., exposure = "incr_exposure") as_triangle(..., development = "dev_m") -> as_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") validate_triangle(..., development = "dev_m") -> validate_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") fit_premium(...) / fit_prem(...) -> fit_exposure(...) fit_lr(...) -> fit_ratio(...) fit_loss(..., prem_method = ..., prem_alpha = ..., prem_fit = ...) -> fit_loss(..., exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ..., exposure_fit = ...) fit_lr(..., prem_method = ..., prem_alpha = ..., prem_regime = ...) -> fit_ratio(..., exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ..., exposure_regime = ...) backtest(..., target = "lr", prem_method = ..., prem_alpha = ...) -> backtest(..., target = "ratio", exposure_method = ..., exposure_alpha = ...) backtest(..., target = "prem") -> backtest(..., target = "exposure") bootstrap(tri, target = "prem") -> bootstrap(tri, target = "exposure") LRFit$prem_alpha, LRFit$prem_regime -> RatioFit$exposure_alpha, $exposure_regime LossFit$prem_fit -> $exposure_fit S3 class "PremFit" -> "ExposureFit" (incl. print.PremFit() / summary.PremFit() -> print.ExposureFit() / summary.ExposureFit()) S3 class "LRFit" -> "RatioFit" (incl. print.LRFit() / summary.LRFit() / plot.LRFit() / plot_triangle.LRFit() -> *.RatioFit()) attr(PremFit_obj, "prem_method") -> attr(ExposureFit_obj, "exposure_method") Triangle / Calendar / Total columns: prem, incr_prem, prem_share, incr_prem_share, lr, incr_lr -> exposure, incr_exposure, exposure_share, incr_exposure_share, ratio, incr_ratio Fit output columns: prem_proj, prem_obs, prem_proc_se, prem_param_se, prem_total_se, prem_total_cv, prem_ci_lo, prem_ci_hi, incr_prem_proj -> exposure_*; lr_proj, lr_se, lr_cv, lr_ci_lo, lr_ci_hi, lr_ult, lr_latest, incr_lr_proj -> ratio_* R source files: R/prem.R, R/lr.R, R/lr-vis.R -> R/exposure.R, R/ratio.R, R/ratio-vis.R Raw dataset (data/experience.rda): column incr_prem -> incr_exposure (regenerated) package name lossratio unchanged; sweep purely code-identifier refactor. conceptual “loss ratio” framework preserved — ratio column / fit name loss--exposure ratio, package continues specialise long-term health insurance reserving developing exposure (risk premium) triangles. Default flip — bootstrap()’s keep_pseudo default changes TRUE FALSE. long-format $pseudo_triangles long-format data.table longer built every call; precomputed $summary (Pythagorean SE decomposition + optional percentile CI) still always present. Skipping long-format reshape saves roughly 250-300 ms ~200 MB typical 4-group monthly triangle B = 999. Users inspect $pseudo_triangles directly pass keep_pseudo = TRUE explicitly; argument unchanged. BREAKING — four constructor functions renamed build_* as_* align tidyverse coercion idiom Python sibling’s lr.Triangle(df) mental model: build_triangle() -> as_triangle() build_calendar() -> as_calendar() build_total() -> as_total() build_link() -> as_link() signature change, verb. Migration global find--replace. functions still validate, coerce, aggregate substantively – as_* name reflects returned object canonical lossratio shape derived raw experience data, just thin type cast. PascalCase classes (Triangle, Calendar, Total, Link) remain unchanged. BREAKING — plot_triangle.Triangle() argument type renamed view parity plot_triangle.CLFit(), plot_triangle.LRFit(), plot_triangle.Backtest(), already used view = c("value", "usage"). type = slot left free plot-method-specific semantics (plot.Backtest(type = "col"/"diag"/"cell"), plot.CLFit(type = "projection"/"reserve"), etc.). Migration: plot_triangle(tri, type = "usage") -> plot_triangle(tri, view = "usage"). BREAKING — backtest() cell-level columns renamed target_actual / target_proj (_incr siblings) actual / expected (actual_incr / expected_incr). new names match actuarial /E convention (aeg = actual - expected, ae_err = actual / expected - 1) self-document role column. Worker-layer column names (target_proj etc. CLFit$full / EDFit$full) unchanged – rename scoped backtest$ae_err. Migration: replace bt$ae_err$target_actual bt$ae_err$actual, bt$ae_err$target_proj bt$ae_err$expected. backtest() result slot fit_fn_name renamed dispatcher clarity (value still dispatcher name — fit_ratio / fit_loss / fit_premium — selected target=). print() / summary() labels updated accordingly. fit_loss(), fit_premium(), fit_ratio(), backtest() now attach $usage data.table result: one row per (group, cohort, dev) cell pre-filter triangle status factor (used / unused / holdout / future). plot_triangle(fit, view = "usage") reads directly instead re-deriving filter logic plot time, heatmap always matches cells fit actually saw. New internal helper .build_usage() packages 2-pass maturity detection plus .compute_triangle_usage() attaches filter metadata (regime / recent / holdout / m_k / m_k_dt) data.table attributes renderer. BREAKING — build_triangle(), build_total(), validate_triangle() rename dev = argument development =. new name explicit development-period axis (matching coh <- cohort symmetry inside function bodies). Migration: replace build_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") build_triangle(..., development = "dev_m"). BREAKING — backtest() result columns renamed value_proj / value_actual (_incr variants) target_proj / target_actual (_incr variants), matching worker-layer target_* generic convention. BREAKING — summary column renames <metric>_<stat> consistency: summary.LossFit / summary.PremiumFit: se_ultimate / cv_ultimate -> ultimate_se / ultimate_cv. .compute_dv() output: median_lr / mad_lr -> lr_median / lr_mad. Internal var_lr (LR variance scratch column) -> lr_var. detect_regime_optimal_window() diagnostics column: mean_magnitude -> magnitude_mean. plot_triangle() now derives axis grain via attr(tri, "grain") raw column name (uy_m, cy_q, …) one package-standard forms, user-supplied names like uym elap_m still render tick labels abbreviated format (23.04, 23.1Q, …). plot_triangle(fit, view = "usage") regime / segment_wise routing fixed: per-group dispatch now honours regime$groups even multi_group = FALSE, regime_at(coverage = "SUR", ...) scopes hline / cohort cut SUR facet . Filtered-cells render unused (gray) instead future (white). .datatable.aware <- TRUE declared R/zzz.R; data.table NSE NOTEs suppressed via mlr3-style (".col") := LHS pattern + reorganised globalVariables() list. Internal temp markers (.col prefix) use function-local NULL bindings per data.table official recommendation. BREAKING — as_experience(), check_experience(), is_experience() removed along Experience S3 class. build_triangle() already validates required columns, coerces dates numerics, aggregates inline, explicit coercion step longer needed (class never required downstream function). Migration: replace exp <- as_experience(df); build_triangle(exp, ...) build_triangle(df, ...). Matches Python sibling 0.0.1.dev7. New fit_intensity() + IntensityFit S3 class (R/intensity.R) — factor-level ED diagnostic, parallel fit_ata() multiplicative side. Returns per-link WLS-estimated intensities g_k standard errors diagnostic stats; projection. ED maturity concept, fit_intensity deliberately omits maturity_args. Link cell-level column g renamed intensity (concept-based, parallels ATA’s ata). Summary / fit per-link output columns (g, g_se, g_var, g_selected) keep Mack-style symbol naming parallelism ATA summary’s f, f_se. Layered naming: cell layer uses concept (intensity), summary layer uses symbol (g). backtest(): cell-level metric aggregation columns renamed aeg ae_err (column ae_err, aggregations ae_err_mean / ae_err_med / ae_err_wt). Print plot labels updated “/E Error”. Formula unchanged: (actual - pred) / pred.

title: lossratio 0.0.0.9000
text: Aggregation: build_triangle() (cohort × dev), build_calendar() (calendar period), build_total() (portfolio total). build_triangle() validates schema coerces required columns inline. Link table: build_link() returns Link object covering single-variable (ATA-style) dual-variable (ED-style) workflows. summary.Link(model = "ata"|"ed") dispatches matching diagnostic. Estimation: fit_ata() (per-link factors ); fit_ed(), fit_cl(), fit_lr() (factors + projection). fit_lr supports three methods — "sa" (default), "ed", "cl". Cell-selection diagnostics: detect_maturity() (dev axis — link beyond ATA factors stable), detect_regime() (cohort axis — structural breaks across underwriting cohorts). Projection diagnostic: detect_convergence() (operates fitted LRFit; valuation depth projected ultimate loss ratio stops revising). Backtest: backtest() (calendar-diagonal hold-, supports fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_lr). Visualisation: S3 plot() plot_triangle() methods every fit class.

text: Seven vignettes covering getting started, aggregation frameworks, chain ladder, loss-ratio projection methods, triangle / ata diagnostics, regime detection, backtesting.
